import React from 'react'
import { View, ActivityIndicator } from 'react-native'
import { useVehicleState, SyncStatus } from '@hooks/useVehicleState'
import { Dashboard } from '@components/Dashboard'

export const HomeScreen = () => {
  const { vehicleState, syncStatus } = useVehicleState()

  if (syncStatus === SyncStatus.PENDING) {
    return <ActivityIndicator size="large" />
  }

  return (
    <View>
      <Dashboard state={vehicleState} syncStatus={syncStatus} />
    </View>
  )
}

Changelog
Agent assisted project updates
Project and initiative updates keep teams aligned, but writing them means pulling out recent changes from issues, documents, and discussions.
Jun 17, 2026
Coding sessions in Linear
Earlier this year, we launched Linear Agent, giving teams a new way to plan and coordinate their issues and projects. Since then, we've added Code Intelligence to extend that understanding to the codebase, and MCP support to bring all of your context together.
Jun 10, 2026
Team documents
Important team context doesn’t always belong in a specific issue, project, or initiative. Teams also need a dedicated place for the notes, docs, and shared references that support their work over time. Now, every team has a page to keep its context organized and easy to access.
Jun 3, 2026
Linear Diffs
Agents generate large volumes of code, but individuals are still accountable for the changes that merge. This leads to a lot of review work, and the capability and performance of traditional review tools hasn't kept up.
May 27, 2026

Project and initiative updates keep teams aligned, but writing them means pulling out recent changes from issues, documents, and discussions.
Instead of finding that context manually, click
Write with Agent
to let Linear do it for you. The agent reviews changes made since the last update, checks messages in the linked Slack channel, and writes an update draft for you to refine.
Add your own perspective by prompting for further changes or editing directly, then publish the update.
Desktop navigation history and pinned tabs
⁠
We’ve rebuilt our desktop tabs to make navigation feel fluid and predictable.
Each desktop tab now has its own history stack, so moving backwards won’t navigate you to another tab
Pinned tabs are now a reliable home for important work. They stay anchored in the tab bar, persist after quitting and reopening the app, and aren’t replaced by new content you open.
These changes are live for current desktop users. Try Linear on desktop by
downloading the app
.
Private sub-teams
⁠
Private sub-teams organize groups that work together under one umbrella, like independent business units, skunkworks efforts, or teams handling sensitive data like People Ops.
When you create a sub-team under a private team, you can now choose between two access options:
Restricted to parent team members
— parent team members can see and choose to join the sub-team. This makes it easy to manage private team structures without coordinating invitations to each sub-team.
Private to team members
— the team is hidden, except to members directly invited to that sub-team
Private sub-teams are available on Business and Enterprise plans. Learn more in our
documentation
.
Build agents with Vercel Eve
⁠
Vercel Eve is an open-source framework for building custom agents.
Build agents that can investigate incidents, monitor SLAs, or analyze customer feedback. You can deploy agents on Vercel with the guided setup, or host them wherever you run your Eve app.
Once connected to Linear, teammates can delegate issues to your agent or mention it in comments. Learn more about Eve in Vercel’s
documentation
.
Release pipeline changelogs
⁠
Releases
let you plan and track your software releases. Now, you can also keep your team aligned on what’s shipping with changelogs for each release pipeline. These changelogs bring a pipeline’s release notes together in one place, so progress is easy to review and share.
Choose to auto-generate release notes in pipeline settings so your changelog is always up to date. Learn more in our
documentation
.
OAuth application manifests
⁠
OAuth application manifests let platforms provide a one-click OAuth app setup experience for users creating integrations.
Use URL parameters to send users directly to a pre-filled
Create OAuth App
form in Linear. An equivalent JSON manifest format lets you store configurations in files, validate them locally, or generate them programmatically.
Find the details in our
developer docs
.

Earlier this year, we launched
Linear Agent
, giving teams a new way to plan and coordinate their issues and projects. Since then, we’ve added
Code Intelligence
to extend that understanding to the codebase, and
MCP support
to bring all of your context together.
Today we’re closing the loop:
Linear Agent can now write code using Claude Code and Codex.
You can triage, plan, review, and ship—all in Linear.
Start a coding session by assigning an issue to Linear, or ask it to make a change in a chat, comment, or Slack thread. Each session is grounded in your workspace context, pulling in issue details, history, customer requests, discussions, and related work.
A coding agent your team will love
⁠
Coding sessions run in the cloud, so you can prototype ideas and make changes without setting up a local development environment.
When the change is ready, Linear returns a new diff for review. Share
preview links
, bring teammates into the discussion, and address feedback with further iterations in the same thread.
Automate the first pass
⁠
When a new issue is reported, Linear Agent can investigate and attempt a fix before it reaches an engineer, reducing the load on your team.
It follows your team’s guidance, gathers evidence from tools like Sentry or Datadog through MCP, and inspects the codebase to trace the root cause before producing a fix.
Linear uses this workflow internally to resolve roughly 30% of incoming bug reports, mostly on the first pass.
You can set up your own agent automation in your team’s Triage settings.
Availability and pricing
⁠
Coding sessions are available on Basic, Business, and Enterprise plans. They require a GitHub connection with code access and use AI credits to run. Workspace admins can enable coding sessions in
Settings
and manage access, usage, and credits from the usage dashboard.
Learn more in the docs
.

Editor
Fixed the tooltip for plain links covering link controls
GitHub
Fixed Linear issues with comma-containing label names failing to sync to GitHub
Initiatives
Fixed Initiative projects table columns occasionally overlapping or taking up too much width
Integrations
Fixed an importer freeze when mapping a large number of users
Issues
Fixed a line break appearing between an issue’s status icon and its identifier in undo/redo toasts
Labels
Archived labels no longer appear in the form-template label group picker
Links
Fixed a crash when a link embed with a missing or invalid URL failed to load a preview
Mobile
Release, Release Note, and Release Pipeline mentions now display properly on mobile
Notifications
Fixed agent completion notifications not being delivered to the user who delegated the work
Projects
Project issue list deep links now redirect to the project overview instead of the filtered issue view
Project Views
Fixed saved project views unexpectedly showing empty groups when grouping and filtering projects
Pulse
Fixed daily Pulse summaries being skipped when a single feed update had a malformed body
SCIM
Fixed SCIM role group pushes not repairing a stale role when the user was  already tracked in the role’s membership
Settings
AI settings dependent on the Linear Agent now show “Disabled” when the Linear Agent is turned off
Slack
Sharing a project comment link in Slack now unfurls the comment instead of the project
SLAs
Fixed missing SLAs for issues created from Slack asks
Updates
Fixed inconsistent icon spacing in the project/initiative update health control
Improvements
Asks
Improved longer, cleaner fallback issue titles when creating Asks from Slack without an AI-generated title
Diffs
The “Copy pull request number” action now copies just the number, without the leading
#
Diffs
Added a file action to open the full file in GitHub from the review diff
Diffs
Guided reviews now jump directly to a selected file instead of scrolling slowly through every file in between
Documents
Added “New document” and “New document from template” to the right click menu on the create issue button
Team Documents
Added cycle documents to the team documents tab, including support for grouping documents by cycle
Triage
On Android, choosing a status that requires a priority to leave triage now opens the priority picker automatically instead of hiding those statuses
Keyboard shortcuts
Editor
Changed the strikethrough shortc

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited members, 2 teams, 250 issues, Agent platform, Linear Agent

tiers.name: Basic
tiers.headline_inclusions: All Free + 5 teams, unlimited issues, unlimited file uploads, admin roles

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: All Basic + unlimited teams, private teams/guests, Triage Intelligence, Insights, Asks, Zendesk/Intercom

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: All Business + SAML/SCIM, granular admin, HIPAA, audit log, priority support, account management

pricing.notes: Basic and Business prices shown as billed yearly; Enterprise is annual billing only. Coding Sessions requires AI credits. Salesforce integration is a paid add-on.

85% of Fortune 100 companies choose Asana¹
AI that works the way your team works
Asana Work Graph®
A neural network of everything your company is doing, every person, task, project, goal, and dependency connected, so humans and agents always know who is doing what, by when, and toward which goal.
Multiplayer
Every workflow, app, and agent runs in a shared space where humans and agents act on the same plan, see the same context, and get smarter with every interaction. Multiple humans can train, guide, and improve any agent.
Shared memory
Shared memory means AI Teammates learn from the work itself. Every completed task, every piece of feedback, every preference gets carried forward, so no one repeats context they've already given and every workflow starts smarter than the last.
Enterprise governance
Every agent has an identity, scoped permissions, an audit trail, and cost constraints, with data access, approved actions, and spend all managed from the same console that governs human users.
AI Teammates
Your team just got bigger
Pre-built AI agents that work alongside your team inside real workflows, with shared memory, governance, and context from the Work Graph, so they are ready to go on day one.
Learn more

Privacy Preference Center
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
Allow All
Manage Consent Preferences
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are strictly necessary to provide you with certain features. For example, these cookies allow you to access secure areas that require registration and set your privacy preferences. Because these cookies are essential to providing services to you, they cannot be disabled. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but it may cause some parts of the site to not work.
Cookies Details
Performance Cookies
Performance Cookies
These cookies allow us or our third-party analytics providers to collect information and statistics on use of our services by you and other visitors. This information helps us to improve our services and products for the benefit of you and others.
Cookies Details
Functional Cookies
Functional Cookies
These cookies provide enhanced functionality, providing chat support, allowing you to more easily complete forms, personalizing content to your preferences, and selecting your communications preferences. If you do not enable these cookies, or choose to disable them in the future, that could impact your ability to use certain features.
Cookies Details
Targeting Cookies
Targeting Cookies
These cookies, provided by our third-party advertising partners, collect information about your browsing habits, as well as your preferences for various features and services. They also provide us with auditing, research, and reporting to know when advertising content has been displayed and how successful the content has been. This information allows us and our third-party advertising providers to display relevant advertising content.

Privacy Preference Center
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
Allow All
Manage Consent Preferences
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are strictly necessary to provide you with certain features. For example, these cookies allow you to access secure areas that require registration and set your privacy preferences. Because these cookies are essential to providing services to you, they cannot be disabled. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but it may cause some parts of the site to not work.
Cookies Details
Performance Cookies
Performance Cookies
These cookies allow us or our third-party analytics providers to collect information and statistics on use of our services by you and other visitors. This information helps us to improve our services and products for the benefit of you and others.
Cookies Details
Functional Cookies
Functional Cookies
These cookies provide enhanced functionality, providing chat support, allowing you to more easily complete forms, personalizing content to your preferences, and selecting your communications preferences. If you do not enable these cookies, or choose to disable them in the future, that could impact your ability to use certain features.
Cookies Details
Targeting Cookies
Targeting Cookies
These cookies, provided by our third-party advertising partners, collect information about your browsing habits, as well as your preferences for various features and services. They also provide us with auditing, research, and reporting to know when advertising content has been displayed and how successful the content has been. This information allows us and our third-party advertising providers to display relevant advertising content.
Cookies Details

analysis.name: Asana
analysis.value_proposition: An AI-native work and project management platform where humans and AI agents collaborate on the same plan via a shared Work Graph.
analysis.ideal_customer: Cross-functional teams at mid-market and enterprise companies (85% of Fortune 100) coordinating projects, portfolios, and AI agents.

features.name: work graph
features.description: Neural network connecting every person, task, project, goal, and dependency across the company.
features.evidence_quote: A neural network of everything your company is doing, every person, task, project, goal, and dependency connected

features.name: multiplayer shared workspace
features.description: Shared space where humans and agents act on the same plan and see the same context.
features.evidence_quote: Every workflow, app, and agent runs in a shared space where humans and agents act on the same plan

features.name: shared memory for ai
features.description: AI Teammates learn from completed tasks, feedback, and preferences carried forward across workflows.
features.evidence_quote: Shared memory means AI Teammates learn from the work itself.

features.name: enterprise ai governance
features.description: Every agent has identity, scoped permissions, audit trail, and cost constraints managed from one console.
features.evidence_quote: Every agent has an identity, scoped permissions, an audit trail, and cost constraints

features.name: ai teammates
features.description: Pre-built AI agents that work alongside teams inside real workflows with shared memory and context.
features.evidence_quote: Pre-built AI agents that work alongside your team inside real workflows

features.name: ai studio
features.description: No-code automation platform for building AI-powered workflows.
features.evidence_quote: AI Studio Basic w/50K credits per billing account per month

features.name: asana dash
features.description: AI Chief of Staff assistant.
features.evidence_quote: Asana Dash — Your AI Chief of Staff

features.name: agentic work management
features.description: Bundle bringing together AI Teammates, AI Studio, Dash, and MCP/AI Connectors for cross-functional teams.
features.evidence_quote: Agentic Work Management brings together AI Teammates, AI Studio, Asana Dash, and MCP and AI Connectors

features.name: asana service management
features.description: AI-native enterprise service management platform for IT, HR, facilities, and legal.
features.evidence_quote: One AI-native enterprise service management platform for IT, HR, facilities, and legal.

features.name: asana client management
features.description: AI-native platform built for agency client work.
features.evidence_quote: Build lasting client relationships on an AI-native platform built for agency work.

features.name: command by asana
features.description: Product surface for developer teams to ship with humans and agents in sync.
features.evidence_quote: Command by Asana — Ship faster with humans and agents in sync.

features.name: stackai by asana
features.description: Workflow automation product for critical business workflows.
features.evidence_quote: StackAI by Asana — For every critical workflow

features.name: mcp connectors
features.description: Model Context Protocol and AI connectors linking agents to workflows.
features.evidence_quote: AI Teammates, AI Studio, Asana Dash, and MCP and AI Connectors

features.name: launch planner agent
features.description: Turns project goals into step-by-step timelines to hit deadlines.
features.evidence_quote: Turns project goals into step-by-step timelines so you can hit every deadline

features.name: workflow optimizer agent
features.description: Finds bottlenecks in workflows and suggests fixes.
features.evidence_quote: Finds bottlenecks in your workflows and suggests fixes so work keeps moving.

features.name: compliance specialist agent
features.description: Reviews project docs against regulatory standards.
features.evidence_quote: Reviews project docs against regulatory standards so teams stay compliant

features.name: status reporter agent
features.description: Turns project updates into executive-ready reports.
features.evidence_quote: Turns project updates into executive-ready reports to keep leaders aligned

features.name: data quality manager agent
features.description: Cleans up missing data and name formatting to keep workspace organized.
features.evidence_quote: Cleans up missing data and name formatting so your workspace stays organized

features.name: custom ai teammates
features.description: Build custom AI agents from a team's collective expertise.
features.evidence_quote: Turn your team's collective expertise into custom AI Teammates

features.name: tasks
features.description: Unlimited tasks for tracking to-dos.
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited tasks and projects

features.name: projects
features.description: Unlimited projects that group tasks around bigger goals.
features.evidence_quote: projects bring them together around bigger goals

features.name: list view
features.description: Structured task list view.
features.evidence_quote: Switch between a structured task list, a kanban-style board, or a calendar view

features.name: board view
features.description: Kanban-style board view.
features.evidence_quote: a kanban-style board

features.name: calendar view
features.description: Calendar view for project scheduling.
features.evidence_quote: a calendar view — all in one project

features.name: timeline view
features.description: Clean simplified scheduling view.
features.evidence_quote: Timeline provides a clean, simplified scheduling view

features.name: gantt view
features.description: Detailed project management view with task hierarchy and progress tracking.
features.evidence_quote: Gantt offers detailed project management features like task hierarchy and progress tracking

features.name: file attachments
features.description: Unlimited file storage with 100MB per-file cap on free tier.
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited storage, 100MB max per file

features.name: status updates
features.description: Regular progress updates on projects.
features.evidence_quote: Keep everyone informed by sharing regular progress updates on your projects

features.name: time tracking integrations
features.description: Connect to external time tracking tools.
features.evidence_quote: Time tracking with integrations

features.name: reporting dashboards
features.description: Visual overview of project progress with charts and metrics.
features.evidence_quote: Get a visual overview of your project's progress with charts and metrics

features.name: automations
features.description: Rules to automate assignments, notifications, and status updates.
features.evidence_quote: Create as many automated rules as you need to handle assignments, notifications

features.name: forms
features.description: Customizable forms that feed information into Asana projects.
features.evidence_quote: Collect information and requests through customizable forms

features.name: custom templates
features.description: Reusable project templates tailored to team processes.
features.evidence_quote: Create reusable templates tailored to your team's processes

features.name: custom fields
features.description: Add extra details like priority, budgets, or approval status to tasks.
features.evidence_quote: Add extra details to your tasks like priority levels, budgets, or approval status

features.name: guest users
features.description: Invite unlimited external collaborators without seat cost.
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited free guests

features.name: portfolios
features.description: Digital folders that organize multiple projects with a high-level view.
features.evidence_quote: a portfolio is a digital folder that organizes multiple projects in one place

features.name: goals
features.description: Track progress on OKRs by connecting tasks to company-wide objectives.
features.evidence_quote: Asana goals help you track progress on what matters most

features.name: workload
features.description: View team workload across projects in a portfolio.
features.evidence_quote: Check workload just for the projects in a single portfolio

features.name: my tasks view
features.description: Personal task inbox aggregating assigned work.
features.evidence_quote: My tasks

features.name: inbox
features.description: Centralized notifications inbox.
features.evidence_quote: Inbox

features.name: messages
features.description: Team messaging within Asana.
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited messages

features.name: admin console
features.description: Console for managing users, permissions, and workspaces.
features.evidence_quote: Manage your workspaces, permissions, and security.

features.name: admin and security
features.description: Enterprise admin and security controls.
features.evidence_quote: Admin and security

features.name: public api
features.description: APIs for building custom apps and solutions.
features.evidence_quote: Asana's APIs let you develop custom solutions

features.name: app rules
features.description: Automate common tasks via app rules.
features.evidence_quote: App Rules to automate common tasks

features.name: app widgets
features.description: See integration info at a glance inside Asana.
features.evidence_quote: App widgets to see info at a glance

features.name: ios app
features.description: Native iOS mobile app.
features.evidence_quote: Download the Asana iOS app

features.name: android app
features.description: Native Android mobile app.
features.evidence_quote: Download the Asana Android app

features.name: desktop app
features.description: Downloadable desktop application.
features.evidence_quote: Download app

features.name: asana gov
features.description: Government-specific offering with dedicated support.
features.evidence_quote: Get Asana Gov support

features.name: project templates
features.description: Library of pre-built project templates.
features.evidence_quote: Project templates

features.name: resource management

Every team starts with Jira
Flexible views, custom workflows, and no-code automation that adapt to how any team works.
Software development
Track bugs, ship releases, skip the status meetings.
Marketing
Plan campaigns, manage creative workflows, and launch on time.
Project management
Manage service requests, track incidents, and keep operations running smoothly.
IT
Coordinate timelines, track deliverables, and ship projects across any team.
Fit for teams big and small
Teams of all types turn teamwork into results with Jira.
Read all stories
Agents in Jira raise the floor for what every team can do. You don't need to be an engineer or an AI expert. If you work in Jira, you get access to the same agentic patterns and automations. That's the real power—capability becomes a property of the system, not just the individual.”
MATTHEW HARGREAVES, Head of Product Delivery and Automation
Read Story
Our whole dev pipeline and planning process runs through [Jira]. It's driving a lot of the business.”
MATT CHESTER, Senior System Engineer
Read Story
[Jira] makes life easier. [We] can spend time on adding value, not busywork.”
JOE COTANT, Senior Technical Program Manager
Read Story
Jira helps our teams stay connected. Plans gives us a single source of truth where we can plan and track different projects across different teams. This brings our non-IT teams into the fold and creates greater visibility and transparency of everyone's work.”
ROB MULVANEY, Sr. Manager - Agile Transformation Office
Read Story
Marketing teams manage their work as sprints in Jira. Sprints aren't just for technical teams – they're for marketing and business teams too.”
SARAH BROWN, Vice President of Marketing
Read Story
One project took 18 months to get up and running. After adopting Atlassian and learning more valuable ways to use the tools, the next one took six months. By tracking everything in Jira and all of our dev tools, we reduced the cost to get the team set up and deliver the product from $2.8 million to $600,000.”
RYAN MOSLEY, Chief of Factory & Engineering Modernization

Manage Preferences
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
More information
Accept all
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Targeting Cookies
Always Active
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.
Functional Cookies
Always Active
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Performance Cookies
Always Active
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
More information
Accept all
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Targeting Cookies
Always Active
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.
Functional Cookies
Always Active
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Performance Cookies
Always Active
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

analysis.name: Jira
analysis.tagline: More projects. More agents. Less chaos.
analysis.value_proposition: AI-era project management that orchestrates across your tech stack, turning plans into shipped work with AI agents and 3K+ integrations.
analysis.ideal_customer: Software, IT, marketing, design, and operations teams at growing companies that need customizable workflows, agent orchestration, and cross-team planning at scale.

features.name: ai agents (rovo)
features.description: Rovo agents use project history and institutional knowledge to deliver outputs across Jira.
features.evidence_quote: Jira agents use your project history and institutional knowledge to deliver real outputs

features.name: rovo search
features.description: AI-powered search across Jira and connected tools.
features.evidence_quote: Rovo Search, Chat, and Agents, and integrated AI-powered work features

features.name: rovo chat
features.description: AI chat interface for teamwork queries.
features.evidence_quote: Rovo Search, Chat, and Agents

features.name: atlassian mcp
features.description: Update work items from Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, GitHub Copilot, or any CLI via the Atlassian MCP.
features.evidence_quote: Update work items directly from Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Github Copilot, or any CLI via the Atlassian MCP

features.name: agent orchestration
features.description: Connect agents from any system and keep humans and agents in sync.
features.evidence_quote: Connect agents from any system and keep humans and agents in sync

features.name: rovo dev
features.description: AI dev agent that can deliver PRs (120 in two weeks per case study).
features.evidence_quote: Delivering 120 PRs in two weeks with Rovo Dev in Jira

features.name: teamwork graph
features.description: Links teams, work, and goals to unlock context in the flow of work.
features.evidence_quote: Link teams, work, and goals to unlock context right in your flow of work

features.name: unlimited goals
features.description: Create goals to align teams and projects to desired outcomes.
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited goals, projects, tasks, and forms

features.name: unlimited projects
features.description: No cap on projects across tiers.
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited goals, projects, tasks, and forms

features.name: unlimited issues
features.description: Track individual pieces of work with no volume cap.
features.evidence_quote: In Jira, teams use issues to track individual pieces of work

features.name: forms
features.description: Drag-and-drop dynamic form builder to collect data.
features.evidence_quote: Create dynamic forms with our drag-and-drop, user-friendly form builder

features.name: backlog view
features.description: Backlog view for planning issues.
features.evidence_quote: Backlog, list, board, timeline, calendar, and summary views

features.name: list view
features.description: List view of work items.
features.evidence_quote: Backlog, list, board, timeline, calendar, and summary views

features.name: board view
features.description: Kanban/scrum board view.
features.evidence_quote: Backlog, list, board, timeline, calendar, and summary views

features.name: timeline view
features.description: Gantt-style timeline view.
features.evidence_quote: Backlog, list, board, timeline, calendar, and summary views

features.name: calendar view
features.description: Calendar view for work items.
features.evidence_quote: Backlog, list, board, timeline, calendar, and summary views

features.name: summary view
features.description: Summary/overview view of a project.
features.evidence_quote: Backlog, list, board, timeline, calendar, and summary views

features.name: reports and dashboards
features.description: Insights on teamwork tracked in any Jira product/project.
features.evidence_quote: Get insights on teamwork tracked in any Jira product or project

features.name: customizable workflows
features.description: Custom workflows to model how a team moves work from to-do to done.
features.evidence_quote: Create a custom workflow that helps your team work as efficiently as possible

features.name: no-code automation
features.description: Automation rules replace manual work; usage caps per tier.
features.evidence_quote: 100 automation rule runs per month … 1,700 … 1,000 per user … Unlimited

features.name: user roles and permissions
features.description: Control what users can create, view, and comment on.
features.evidence_quote: Control what users can create, view, and comment on in Jira

features.name: external collaboration
features.description: Give people outside your org anonymous access to Jira projects.
features.evidence_quote: Give people outside your org anonymous access to Jira projects

features.name: multi-region data residency
features.description: Specify where content and data are hosted.
features.evidence_quote: Specify where your content and data are hosted

features.name: cross-team planning
features.description: Plan and track goals, projects, and dependencies across teams (Plans).
features.evidence_quote: Plan and track goals, projects, and dependencies in one place

features.name: dependency management
features.description: Track dependencies across projects and teams.
features.evidence_quote: Cross-team planning and dependency management

features.name: approval workflows
features.description: Add customizable approval steps to workflows.
features.evidence_quote: Add approval steps to your workflows to get required signoffs

features.name: customizable onboarding
features.description: Personalized onboarding messaging and resources by team type.
features.evidence_quote: Welcome new users with personalized messaging and resources

features.name: unlimited storage
features.description: No storage cap on Premium and Enterprise.
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited storage

features.name: uptime sla
features.description: 99.9% (Premium) and 99.95% (Enterprise) uptime SLAs.
features.evidence_quote: 99.9% uptime SLA … 99.95% uptime SLA

features.name: atlassian analytics
features.description: Cross-product insights with Atlassian Analytics and Data Lake.
features.evidence_quote: Cross-product insights with Atlassian Analytics and Data Lake

features.name: advanced admin controls
features.description: Advanced governance controls including advanced encryption and Shadow IT controls.
features.evidence_quote: Manage users and security at scale with advanced governance controls

features.name: sso
features.description: Enforce SSO via Atlassian Guard Standard.
features.evidence_quote: plus SCIM, enforce SSO, and more with Atlassian Guard Standard included

features.name: scim
features.description: SCIM user provisioning.
features.evidence_quote: plus SCIM, enforce SSO

features.name: multiple idp support
features.description: Support for multiple identity providers in central admin console.
features.evidence_quote: Get support for multiple identity providers (IdPs) within your central admin console

features.name: multiple sites
features.description: Up to 150 sites for data isolation and administrative autonomy.
features.evidence_quote: Multiple sites (up to 150)

features.name: 24/7 support
features.description: 24/7 support for critical issues (Premium) or all issues (Enterprise).
features.evidence_quote: 24/7 support for critical issues … 24/7 support for all issues

features.name: advanced encryption
features.description: Advanced encryption controls for enterprise data.
features.evidence_quote: advanced governance controls like advanced encryption

features.name: shadow it controls
features.description: Detect and manage unsanctioned app usage.
features.evidence_quote: advanced encryption and Shadow IT controls

features.name: 3000+ integrations
features.description: Slack, GitHub, Figma, Google Docs, and 3K+ third-party integrations.
features.evidence_quote: With 3K+ integrations … Slack, Github, Figma, Google Docs, all connect seamlessly

features.name: marketplace apps
features.description: Atlassian Marketplace ecosystem for extending Jira.
features.evidence_quote: Explore apps for Jira | Atlassian Marketplace

features.name: jira service management
features.description: Service management, incident, and customer support product line.
features.evidence_quote: Jira Service Management templates

features.name: software project templates
features.description: Prebuilt templates for software development teams.
features.evidence_quote: Software Development … templates

features.name: business team templates
features.description: Templates for Marketing, HR, Legal, Finance, Sales, Operations, Design, IT.
features.evidence_quote: Software Development, Finance, Marketing, Design, Sales, Operations, Service Management, HR, Legal, IT Operations

features.name: agile boards
features.description: Scrum and kanban agile boards.
features.evidence_quote: Agile Project Management

features.name: program management
features.description: Program-level planning across projects.
features.evidence_quote: Program Management

features.name: campaign management
features.description: Marketing campaign planning workflows.
features.evidence_quote: Campaign Management

features.name: bug tracking
features.description: Track bugs and ship releases.
features.evidence_quote: Track bugs, ship releases, skip the status meetings

features.name: incident tracking
features.description: Track incidents and service requests.
features.evidence_quote: Manage service requests, track incidents

features.name: regional support
features.description: 9/5 regional support with 2-hour response for critical issues.
features.evidence_quote: 9/5 regional support … 2-hr response time for critical issues

features.name: atlassian community support
features.description: Peer support from Atlassian Community.
features.evidence_quote: Find answers and support from other Atlassian users

features.name: weekly release notes digest
features.description: Weekly Cloud Release Notes email digest.
features.evidence_quote: To get a weekly digest of these Cloud Release Notes

features.name: confluence integration
features.description: Native integration with Confluence document collaboration.
features.evidence_quote: Confluence – Document collaboration

features.name: bitbucket integration
features.description: Native integration with Bitbucket git code management.
features.evidence_quote: Bitbucket – Git code management

features.name: cloud migration
features.description: Cloud Migration Center for moving from Data Center to Cloud.
features.evidence_quote: Cloud Migration Center

features.name: data center deployment
features.description: Self-managed Data Center deployment option.
features.evidence_quote: Jira Software … Cloud … Data Center

differentiators: AI-era project management with native agents (Rovo) that use project history for real outputs
Atlassian MCP lets any CLI/AI tool (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Copilot) update work items
3,000+ integrations and Atlassian Marketplace ecosystem — largest in PM category
Teamwork Graph linking teams, work, and goals across the Atlassian suite
Named Leader in 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for DevOps Platforms and Collaborative Work Management
Flexible enough to serve engineering, IT, marketing, ops, HR, legal from one platform

praised: 'Our whole dev pipeline and planning process runs through Jira. It's driving a lot of the business.' (Matt Chester)
'Agents in Jira raise the floor for what every team can do… capability becomes a property of the system, not just the individual.' (Matthew Hargreaves)
'[Jira] makes life easier. [We] can spend time on adding value, not busywork.' (Joe Cotant)
'Plans gives us a single source of truth where we can plan and track different projects across different teams.' (Rob Mulvaney)
Massive third-party ecosystem via Marketplace (many 4.5+ star apps with tens of thousands of installs)
complaints: 'JIRA is an antipattern' (HN, 1012 upvotes / 367 comments) — recurring criticism of process overhead
'I fucking hate Jira' (HN, 971 upvotes / 526 comments) — general UX/complexity frustration
'Why Jira Sucks' and 'Why Fogbugz lost to Jira' — narratives about bloat and enterprise-driven design
Steady demand for lightweight alternatives (Plane, Tara appearing repeatedly as HN Show/Launch posts)
Security incident referenced: 'Misconfigured Jira instances exposed data of hundreds of Fortune 500 companies'

free_tier.limits: Up to 10 users, 2 GB storage, 100 automation rule runs/month

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited goals/projects/tasks/forms, all views, dashboards, 2 GB, up to 10 users

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.headline_inclusions: Rovo AI, roles/permissions, external collab, data residency, 250 GB, up to 100K users

tiers.name: Premium
tiers.headline_inclusions: Cross-team planning, dependencies, approvals, unlimited storage, 99.9% SLA, 24/7 support

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Analytics + Data Lake, advanced admin/security, SSO/SCIM/multi-IdP, up to 150 sites, 99.95% SLA

pricing.notes: Standard/Premium seat prices shown are annual-billing effective rates (up to 17% off monthly). Free tier hard-capped at 10 users. Enterprise is annual-only. Automation rule runs are metered per tier (100 → 1,700 → 1,000/user → unlimited). Atlassian Guard Standard bundled with Enterprise for identity/security.

analysis.name: Monday.com
analysis.value_proposition: An AI work platform where people and agents drive results together across projects, CRM, dev, marketing, and IT service.
analysis.ideal_customer: Cross-functional teams at SMB through Fortune 500 companies looking for a configurable work OS spanning PMO, marketing, ops, IT, HR, sales, and product/dev

free_tier.limits: Up to 2 seats, 3 boards, 3 docs, 8 column types

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Up to 2 seats, 3 boards, 3 docs, 200+ templates, iOS/Android apps

tiers.name: Basic
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited free viewers, unlimited items, Sidekick, Vibe builder, 1,000 AI credits/mo

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.headline_inclusions: Automations & integrations (250/mo), timeline/Gantt/calendar, guest access, notetaker, AI columns

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.headline_inclusions: 25k automations/integrations, private boards, advanced views, time tracking, AI workflow builder

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Custom pricing, custom AI credits, advanced security, discounts available

pricing.notes: Seat-based; yearly billing saves 18%; AI credits bundled per tier (1k/2k/3k/custom) with add-on packs; API call limits scale by tier (1k/day Standard, 10k/day Pro); special promotional $10-$30/mo discounts shown on pricing page

Growth & Acquisition, Week of Apr 21
Paid ROAS holding at 4.2x across search, up from 3.8x last week. Activation rate hit 38%, up 4pts month-over-month — best since December.
Growth & Acquisition Update for the Week of April 21
This week, we are excited to report that our Paid Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) has shown remarkable strength, now standing at an impressive 4.2x in search. This marks a significant increase from last week's figure of 3.8x, indicating that our advertising strategies are effectively resonating with our target audience. Additionally, our activation rate has reached 38%, which is a notable rise of 4 points compared to last month. This is particularly encouraging as it represents the highest activation rate we have seen since December, showcasing our ongoing efforts to enhance user engagement and conversion.

Growth & Acquisition, Week of Apr 21
Paid ROAS holding at 4.2x across search, up from 3.8x last week. Activation rate hit 38%, up 4pts month-over-month — best since December.
Growth & Acquisition Update for the Week of April 21
This week, we are excited to report that our Paid Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) has shown remarkable strength, now standing at an impressive 4.2x in search. This marks a significant increase from last week's figure of 3.8x, indicating that our advertising strategies are effectively resonating with our target audience. Additionally, our activation rate has reached 38%, which is a notable rise of 4 points compared to last month. This is particularly encouraging as it represents the highest activation rate we have seen since December, showcasing our ongoing efforts to enhance user engagement and conversion.

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
More information
Allow All
Manage Consent Preferences
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Performance Cookies
Always Active
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
Functional Cookies
Always Active
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Targeting Cookies
Always Active
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

Welcome to the future of productivity, where useful features and integrations are shipped to you weekly.
Start integrating
<
Enter terms to filter:
Filter
Native integrations
Automatic Import
More Integrations
Native integrations
Built by ClickUp, loved by everyone.
ClickUp API
Build custom ClickUp integrations and apps using the public API for tailored automation and data synchronization.
Slack
Create and update ClickUp Tasks from Slack messages, unfurl links with rich details and actions, and receive real-time task updates in Slack channels.
GitHub
Sync commits, branches, pull requests, and issues between GitHub and ClickUp to track development progress without switching tools.
Zapier
Automate workflows between ClickUp and thousands of apps by setting triggers and actions, no coding required.
GitLab
Link GitLab commits, branches, pull requests, and issues with ClickUp Tasks for real-time visibility and streamlined development.
Webhooks
Send real-time ClickUp data to external apps and services by triggering automated workflows through Webhooks.
HubSpot
Sync CRM data, tickets, and properties between ClickUp and HubSpot to track pipeline progress and manage projects seamlessly.
Everhour

differentiators: Software to replace all software — one platform for projects, chat, docs, AI, and 100+ productivity features
Brain² — every frontier AI model (GPT, Claude Opus, Gemini) included in one subscription with full workspace context
Multi-model AI with team-shared memory and preferences (personality, shorthand, tone)
Super Agents — AI agents that execute cross-tool, cross-team workflows with full context
Any-MCP support — connects to Google Drive, GitHub, Salesforce and any MCP-compatible tool
Super Fair Billing — AI cost optimizations passed through to customers; providers subsidized during rate spikes
Unlimited free plan members with unlimited tasks on the free tier
100% money-back guarantee on paid plans

praised: All-in-one consolidation replacing many SaaS tools (Slack, Notion, Loom, Asana, Google Drive) with claimed ~$282K/yr savings for 500-person company
Free forever plan with unlimited tasks and unlimited free members
Rapid feature velocity — 'useful features and integrations are shipped to you weekly'
Multi-model AI (GPT/Claude/Gemini) unified under one subscription with workspace context
complaints: Users building their own PM tool 'after trying Trello, Asana, ClickUp, etc.' (HN 632↑, 453c) — implies dissatisfaction with existing PM tools including ClickUp
'22% Layoff at ClickUp' / 'Clickup Reduced Headcount by 22%' — HN posts raise concerns about company stability
'How can a product like ClickUp grow so fast (or even grow at all)?' — HN skepticism about product quality vs. growth
Security concern: 'Clickup.com page source contains hardcoded Split.io API key – data leak' (HN 4↑)
Feature bloat / 'making a cool ClickUp/Asana alternative' — HN 7↑ signals market perception of overwhelming surface area

free_tier.limits: 60MB storage, 1 form, unlimited tasks, unlimited free plan members

tiers.name: Free Forever
tiers.headline_inclusions: 60MB storage, unlimited tasks/members, 2FA, docs, kanban, sprints, calendar, 1 form, 24/7 support

tiers.name: Unlimited
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited storage/integrations/gantt/custom fields, native time tracking, goals, chat, email

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited dashboards, 5K automations/mo, mind maps, private whiteboards, Google SSO, sprint points

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: SAML SSO/SCIM, audit log, 250K automations/mo, custom branding, HIPAA, data residency, CSM

tiers.name: Brain AI (add-on) — Free Forever
tiers.headline_inclusions: Trial access to advanced AI features across chat, tasks, docs

tiers.name: Brain AI (add-on)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited Brain Assistant/@Brain Agent/AI chat, Claude+GPT+Gemini, enterprise search, 1,500 credits/mo

tiers.name: Everything AI (add-on)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited ambient answers, notetaker, image gen, AI fields, 3x Super Agents, 5,000 credits/mo

pricing.notes: Yearly billing saves up to 30% on core plans and 20% on AI plans. AI is a separate add-on ($9 or $28 per user/mo) on top of the base plan. AI Super Credits are metered at $0.001 each ($10 per 10,000). Workspace-wide upgrades required (cannot upgrade individual users). 100% money-back guarantee. ClickUp Assist add-on provides 2 hrs/mo of 1:1 AI setup expert time.

May 26, 2026

Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Finally! Merge cells in simple tables.
Create headers that span multiple columns or group related rows into structured layouts.
Give it a try: select multiple cells → open the cell menu → select
Merge cells
.
See the
announcement
on X →
May 13, 2026
3.5
3.5: Notion Developer Platform
Notion? For developers? Fair question. It’s true, we haven’t always been the most developer-focused platform. Today that changes.
Introducing: the
Notion Developer Platform.
Now you (and your coding agents) can write code to sync any data and build any agent tool, all running on our infrastructure. You can also bring your favorite agents into Notion (like Claude, Codex, Decagon, or ones you’ve built yourself). It turns Notion into one shared canvas with all your data, for your team and agents to work together.
Learn more →
Orchestrate all your team’s agents (Alpha)
Bring your favorite agents into Notion with the External Agents API, even the ones you built yourself. We’ve also partnered with Claude, Codex, Decagon, and more so they work out of the box. Now Notion is your orchestration layer: a Decagon ticket routes to your coding agent, which proposes a fix and loops in your team to approve. Anyone can work with agents in Notion, not just engineers.
Join the External Agents waitlist →
Notion is our AI layer because it’s where work is created or imagined—and we want our agents as close to the action as possible.
Dan Gilbert
CEO at Brainlabs
Sync
any
data source (Beta)
Sync any data source with an API into your Notion databases. No servers for your team to manage. Our new database sync is powered by Workers that run on our infrastructure (more on this below). Pull in tickets from Zendesk so agents can take a first pass on the fix. Sync customer data from Salesforce for agents to build detailed reports. Connect Strava and Spotify data to curate the perfect running playlist. Whatever context you need can now live in Notion.
Watch the demo →
Workers give us the tools to build deep integrations into Notion that simply couldn't exist before. We have a worker that runs every night that syncs and converts uneditable PDFs in our Google Drive into rich, fully editable pages in an organized Notion database. It unlocks this data for our team and agents - and saves us tons of time.
Sam Lambert
CEO at PlanetScale
Build custom tools for your agents (Beta)
Give your Custom Agents capabilities that Notion and MCP don’t cover on their own. Write your logic in code and deploy it as a Worker. It’s
deterministic
, so it’s more reliable than LLM reasoning, and a
fraction of the token cost
. Use them to generate assets, query internal data, or take action in any other app.
Read the docs →
I think of Notion Workers as infrastructure: they auto-populate, auto-update, and set up the systems I need.
Austin Tedesco
Head of Growth at Every
Trigger Notion workflows from anywhere (Beta)
Webhooks used to be a one-way street: Notion could trigger your other apps, but not the other way around. Now any app can trigger Notion directly. A Worker receives the webhook, runs your logic, and takes action in Notion or calls other APIs. Use it to close tasks when a PR merges, update your CRM when a subscription changes, or create an onboarding doc when an offer is signed.
Read the docs →
Meet your Notion Workers
Database sync, agent tools, and webhook triggers are all powered by a new primitive we’re calling Workers. Notion Workers are our hosted runtime for custom code, so you can extend Notion without running your own servers. You and your coding agent write the code, deploy it through the CLI, and run it in a secure sandbox. Workers are free to try during the beta period. Starting August 11 2026, Workers will run on Notion credits.
Read the docs →
Workers let us connect directly to other tools’ APIs and automate what used to be manual handoffs. Notion becomes the connective layer, and Workers fill in whatever gaps exist between your tools.
Brian Emerick
Technical Program Manager at Vercel
A Notion CLI, built for devs and coding agents
The Notion command-line interface (CLI), made specifically for developers and coding agents, is a new way to work with Notion programmatically. Use it to sign in to your workspace, read and take action in Notion, build and deploy Workers, and extend Notion however your team needs. To install, run curl -fsSL https://ntn.dev | bash.
Watch the demo →
Use your Notion Agents in any app (Alpha)
Soon, your Notion Agents won’t have to stay in Notion. With the Notion Agent SDK, you can embed an agent inside your other tools. Trigger a deal report from a button in your CRM. Answer repeat questions inside MS Teams or Discord with verified knowledge from your workspace. Or pull customer context into Amplitude, Hex, or any dashboard.
Join the Agent SDK waitlist →
Manage all connections from one tab
We’ve updated the Connections tab in workspace settings. Now, every connection lives in one place, so your team can see everything that’s available at a glance. It includes personal and workspace connections, personal access tokens for API authentication, and internal API connections. And each app shows every connection type in one listing. Go to
Settings
→
Connections
to check it out (or
click here
).
Agents “hall of fame”
Knowing what agents to build can be the hardest part, so we pulled together the best agents from companies like Ramp, Clay, and Vercel into one library. Each one comes with a checklist of exactly what you need (databases, pages, tools) and a starter prompt to copy/paste. Pick one and set it up in minutes.
Browse the collection →
More updates your devs will love
Markdown API:
ICYMI read and write Notion pages as Markdown. Built for the way agents already think.
Notion MCP:
Now works with Meeting Notes and block comments, plus creating and updating databases are 91% more token-efficient.
Notion API:
Any member can build connections (not just Workspace Owners). Plus workspace-scoped OAuth and personal access tokens.
See releases →
Developer Portal:
app.notion.com/developers
is now a dedicated portal for creating, managing, and listing your connections and tokens.
Developer Docs:
Rebuilt and streamlined for clarity with a built-in AI assistant to help you find what you need.
This is just the beginning for the
Notion Developer Platform
. Any data, any tool, any agent, all running on our infrastructure. We can’t wait to see what you build.
Keep the feedback coming!
Ivan
P.S. We announced all of this and more at Make with Notion: Developer Platform.
Watch the keynote →
P.P.S. Curious what teams are already building? See how
Every
,
Brainlabs
, and
Vercel
are using our developer platform in production.
P.P.P.S. One more thing. You can now merge cells in simple tables, just like a spreadsheet. We’re excited about this one too.
May 7, 2026
Plan Mode
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
When you ask your agent to make significant changes like rewriting pages or updating databases in bulk, results can drift off course if your intent is unclear. Plan Mode introduces a preliminary step where your agent asks clarifying questions and builds a detailed plan before acting. The result is fewer surprises and more confidence in complex, multi-step work.
Learn more about
Plan Mode
from the Help Center →
May 6, 2026
New Custom Agent Directory
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Custom Agents now have a dedicated place in your Library. Use the Custom Agent Directory to browse all your workspace’s agents, pin your favorites, and create new ones to automate your team’s busywork 24/7.
Get started from the sidebar:
Library
→
Agents
.
May 5, 2026
New Custom Agent controls for admins
Two months ago, we launched Custom Agents in beta. Since then, teams have created more than a million of them and honestly pushed them further than we expected!
One theme from admins was consistent:
as Custom Agents scaled, so did the need for more control and visibility.
Your feedback shaped what we built next, so here’s what's new.
Set guardrails before Custom Agents run
Managing who can create Custom Agents and how much each agent is allowed to spend make rollouts easier to manage:
Start by choosing who create agents, either individuals or user groups, then expand access over time as you build confidence. You can do this in
Settings
→
Notion AI
→
Agents
.
To keep spend predictable, set per-agent credit limits with agent creators. If you’re on Enterprise and planning a broader rollout, you can also set one workspace-level credit limit that applies to all new and existing agents. Both controls are in your usage dashboard at
Settings
→
Notion credits
.
Track every agent and spend across your workspace
The Notion credits dashboard shows total usage broken down by agent, plus spend trends, status, and recent activity.
If an agent is pacing higher than expected, you can disable it right away in
Settings
→
Notion AI
→
Agents
, then work with the creator to adjust it before turning it back on.
If you manage multiple workspaces, you’ll also see a credits dashboard in your
admin console
to track credit pacing and Custom Agent usage across your organization.
Scale agents with built-in guardrails
A few things happen automatically to keep rollouts predictable even at scale:
Get notified by email and in-app as your workspace approaches its credit limit, so you’ve time to plan ahead.
If credits run out, all agents pause until you add more, so you won’t get surprise bills.
If a new agent starts spending unusually fast, we’ll pause it and notify the creator to review.
Members can request a higher credit limit, but nothing goes through without admin approval.
May 4, 2026
A new home on mobile
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Navigating Notion on mobile just got easier with the new home tab. Now your home, chats, meetings, and inbox are just one swipe away.
See the
announcement
on X →
May 1, 2026
Add Custom Agents to private Slack channels
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Custom Agents can now read and reply in private Slack channels. Get started from your workspace settings under
Settings
→
Notion AI
→
AI connectors
→
Enable access to private content
. Custom Agents only see the private channels you invite them to.
Learn more about our
Slack AI Connector
in our Help Center →
April 27, 2026
Rollups now as currency, percent, and more
Rollups now support number formatting (USD, EUR, percent, etc.) and decimal places. Show budget totals, conversion rates, and more in your rollups without any formula workarounds.
Learn more about
Relations & rollups
in our Help Center →
April 24, 2026
Find pages faster from the breadcrumb
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Hover over any item in the breadcrumb to see sibling pages, including pages inside databases. This makes it easier to find related pages and navigate your workspace.
See the
announcement
on X →
April 17, 2026
Mail & Calendar, now in your settings
Connect your calendar and inbox from a new dedicated tab in your settings. After linking accounts, Notion AI can handle work across your apps like scheduling meetings and drafting emails.
