analysis.name: Notion
analysis.tagline: Where teams and agents ship together.
analysis.value_proposition: AI-native workspace that unifies docs, projects, knowledge, and custom agents so teams can capture context, find answers, and automate tasks.
analysis.ideal_customer: Cross-functional teams (eng, product, design, marketing, IT) at startups through Fortune 100 who want a single AI-native workspace to replace docs, wikis, and lightweight PM tools.

features.name: docs
features.description: Collaborative documents with blocks and rich formatting
features.evidence_quote: Docs — Simple and powerful.

features.name: knowledge base
features.description: Central wiki/source of truth for teams and agents
features.evidence_quote: Knowledge Base — One source of truth for teams and agents.

features.name: projects
features.description: Project and task management with databases
features.evidence_quote: Projects — Less tracking. More progress.

features.name: databases
features.description: Structured databases with subtasks, dependencies, custom properties
features.evidence_quote: Databases including subtasks, dependencies, custom properties and more

features.name: sub-tasks
features.description: Nested tasks within database items
features.evidence_quote: including subtasks, dependencies

features.name: dependencies
features.description: Task dependency relationships
features.evidence_quote: including subtasks, dependencies

features.name: custom properties
features.description: User-defined database columns/properties
features.evidence_quote: custom properties and more

features.name: database views
features.description: Multiple views over the same database
features.evidence_quote: Database views

features.name: notion ai
features.description: AI features for drafting, summarizing, autofilling
features.evidence_quote: Notion AI — AI tools for work

features.name: custom agents
features.description: Build AI agents to automate repetitive team work
features.evidence_quote: Custom Agents — Automate repetitive work for your team.

features.name: notion agent
features.description: On-demand agent that completes multi-step tasks
features.evidence_quote: Notion Agent — Completes complex, multi-step tasks

features.name: q&a agents
features.description: Agents that answer questions from existing knowledge
features.evidence_quote: Q&A agents — Answers questions instantly using knowledge you already have.

features.name: task routing agents
features.description: Agents that assign, prioritize, and route tasks
features.evidence_quote: Task routing agents — Assigns, prioritizes, and routes tasks on its own.

features.name: reporting agents
features.description: Agents that summarize, write, and send reports
features.evidence_quote: Reporting agents — Summarizes, writes, and sends reports for you.

features.name: ai meeting notes
features.description: Auto-generated meeting notes and follow-ups
features.evidence_quote: AI Meeting Notes — Perfectly written by AI

features.name: enterprise search
features.description: Search across Notion and connected apps
features.evidence_quote: Search across connected apps like Slack, Github & more

features.name: external agents api
features.description: Bring third-party agents (Claude, Codex, Decagon) into Notion
features.evidence_quote: Bring your favorite agents into Notion with the External Agents API

features.name: agent sdk
features.description: Embed Notion Agents in other apps
features.evidence_quote: With the Notion Agent SDK, you can embed an agent inside your other tools.

features.name: workers
features.description: Hosted runtime for custom code to extend Notion
features.evidence_quote: Notion Workers are our hosted runtime for custom code

features.name: database sync
features.description: Sync external data sources into Notion databases
features.evidence_quote: Sync any data source with an API into your Notion databases.

features.name: webhooks
features.description: Trigger Notion workflows from external apps
features.evidence_quote: Trigger Notion workflows from anywhere

features.name: notion cli
features.description: Command-line interface for developers and coding agents
features.evidence_quote: A Notion CLI, built for devs and coding agents

features.name: notion calendar
features.description: Standalone calendar app tied to Notion
features.evidence_quote: Notion Calendar — It's time

features.name: notion mail
features.description: Notion's email client
features.evidence_quote: Notion Mail

features.name: notion sites
features.description: Publish Notion pages as public websites
features.evidence_quote: Basic sites — Publish a Notion page to the web

features.name: custom sites
features.description: Custom branding, themes, favicon, Google Analytics on sites
features.evidence_quote: Customize your top header, choose a light or dark theme, add a custom favicon

features.name: forms
features.description: Basic forms for capturing responses in Notion
features.evidence_quote: Basic forms — Capture, organize, and act on responses right inside Notion.

features.name: custom forms
features.description: Forms without Notion branding
features.evidence_quote: Custom forms — Remove Notion branding from public forms

features.name: charts
features.description: Data visualizations (donut, bar, line) from databases
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited charts — Visualize data from databases (e.g. donut, bar, and line charts).

features.name: blocks
features.description: Composable content blocks on any page
features.evidence_quote: Blocks are pieces of content you add to a page

features.name: file uploads
features.description: Attach images, videos, files to pages
features.evidence_quote: you can upload images, videos and file attachments up to 5MB each

features.name: page history
features.description: Version history and page restore
features.evidence_quote: Restore your page to a previous version.

features.name: merge cells
features.description: Merge cells in simple tables for structured layouts
features.evidence_quote: Merge cells in simple tables

features.name: comments
features.description: Inline comments and collaboration
features.evidence_quote: Comments & reminders — Collaborate with others using comments and reminders.

features.name: reminders
features.description: Time-based reminders on pages/tasks
features.evidence_quote: Comments & reminders

features.name: guests
features.description: Guest collaborators with limited access
features.evidence_quote: Members vs. guests

features.name: teamspaces
features.description: Group workspaces for teams
features.evidence_quote: Create teamspaces that can't be seen or discovered

features.name: private teamspaces
features.description: Hidden teamspaces for sensitive info
features.evidence_quote: Private teamspaces — teamspaces that can't be seen or discovered

features.name: granular database permissions
features.description: Row-level access limited to assigned collaborators
features.evidence_quote: Granular database permissions — Limit access to rows where the collaborator is assigned.

features.name: verified pages
features.description: Verified badge on up-to-date pages, surfaces in search/AI citations
features.evidence_quote: Verify any page — Add a verified badge to pages that are up to date.

features.name: sso
features.description: SAML single sign-on for user access
features.evidence_quote: SAML SSO — Manage employee access at scale with secure single sign-on.

features.name: domain verification
features.description: Verify email domain ownership for security features
features.evidence_quote: Domain verification — Verify ownership of an email domain

features.name: domain management
features.description: Manage all workspaces on a verified domain (transfer, delete, claim)
features.evidence_quote: Domain management — take actions on workspaces that belong to your verified domain

features.name: scim provisioning
features.description: User/group provisioning via SCIM API
features.evidence_quote: User provisioning (SCIM) — Access the Notion SCIM API

features.name: audit log
features.description: Detailed activity log for security and compliance
features.evidence_quote: Audit log — Access a detailed log of security and safety-related activity

features.name: advanced security controls
features.description: Prevent external sharing, disable guests, workspace policies
features.evidence_quote: Advanced security & controls — prevent certain people from sharing pages externally

features.name: zero data retention
features.description: LLM providers do not retain data for Enterprise
features.evidence_quote: our LLM providers utilize zero data retention for Enterprise plan workspaces

features.name: dlp integration
features.description: Data loss prevention connections
features.evidence_quote: Security & Compliance connections (DLP, SIEM)

features.name: siem integration
features.description: SIEM tool connections for centralized audit logs
features.evidence_quote: Connect to SIEM and DLP tools to automatically centralize audit logs

features.name: customer success manager
features.description: Dedicated CSM for enterprise accounts
features.evidence_quote: Customer success manager — Our team of Customer Success Managers

features.name: web clipper
features.description: Browser extension to clip web pages into Notion
features.evidence_quote: Web Clipper

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native iOS and Android apps
features.evidence_quote: iOS & Android

features.name: desktop apps
features.description: Native Mac and Windows apps
features.evidence_quote: Mac & Windows

features.name: templates marketplace
features.description: 100k+ community templates across life, work, PM, school
features.evidence_quote: Life 33410 templates … Work 24118 templates

features.name: public api
features.description: Public developer API for integrations
features.evidence_quote: Public API

features.name: ai connectors
features.description: AI-native connectors for Jira, Linear, Teams, Outlook, Calendar
features.evidence_quote: Notion AI Connector for MS Teams — AI connector

features.name: automations
features.description: Rule-based automations in databases and pages
features.evidence_quote: Automations

features.name: import export
features.description: Import from and export to other tools/formats
features.evidence_quote: Import & export your data

features.name: notion academy
features.description: Learning platform with certifications and badges
features.evidence_quote: Notion Academy — learn Notion, build smarter workflows, and validate your skills with badges & certifications

differentiators: AI-native workspace where teams and agents work together in one shared canvas
G2's #1 knowledge base for 3 consecutive years
Custom Agents you can build for any repeating work (Q&A, task routing, reporting)
Notion Workers: hosted runtime for custom code with deterministic tools at a fraction of LLM token cost
External Agents API — bring Claude, Codex, Decagon, or your own agents into Notion as an orchestration layer
Trusted by 98% of Forbes Cloud 100, 62% of Fortune 100, over 50% of YC companies, 100M users

recent_ships.what: Merge cells in simple tables — headers spanning columns and grouped rows
recent_ships.source: May 26, 2026 — Merge cells in simple tables

recent_ships.what: Notion 3.5: Developer Platform with Workers, CLI, external agents
recent_ships.source: May 13, 2026 — 3.5: Notion Developer Platform

recent_ships.what: External Agents API (Alpha) — bring Claude, Codex, Decagon into Notion
recent_ships.source: Orchestrate all your team's agents (Alpha)

recent_ships.what: Database sync from any API source (Beta)
recent_ships.source: Sync any data source (Beta)

recent_ships.what: Custom Worker tools for Custom Agents (Beta)
recent_ships.source: Build custom tools for your agents (Beta)

recent_ships.what: Inbound webhooks — trigger Notion workflows from anywhere (Beta)
recent_ships.source: Trigger Notion workflows from anywhere (Beta)

recent_ships.what: Notion CLI for developers and coding agents
recent_ships.source: A Notion CLI, built for devs and coding agents

recent_ships.what: Notion Agent SDK (Alpha) — embed agents in other apps
recent_ships.source: Use your Notion Agents in any app (Alpha)

recent_ships.what: Unified Connections tab in workspace settings
recent_ships.source: Manage all connections from one tab

praised: All-in-one workspace for notes, tasks, wikis, and databases (HN: 'Notion – All-in-one workspace for notes, tasks, wikis, and databases')
Thoughtful design speeds up collaboration and decisions (customer quote, Renee Solorzano)
Custom Agents let teams build AI tools that do the work for them (Ben Levick, Head of Ops)
Developer Platform / Workers praised as infrastructure that auto-populates and auto-updates (Austin Tedesco, Every)
complaints: Sufficient interest in open-source alternatives — 'Docs – Open source alternative to Notion or Outline' (1952↑) and 'Focalboard – a self-hosted alternative to Trello, Notion, and Asana' (694↑)
Data lock-in / access concerns — HN 'Ask HN: Notion is withholding my company data, what can I do?' (479↑)
Scaling pain — Postgres sharding lessons published (HN 'Lessons learned from sharding Postgres at Notion')

free_tier.limits: Unlimited pages for individuals, limited blocks for 2+ members, 5MB per file upload, 7-day page history, trial of Notion AI

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Individuals: unlimited pages, trial of Notion AI, basic forms, basic sites, Notion Calendar, databases

tiers.name: Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: Small teams: unlimited blocks/files, custom forms/sites, unlimited charts, basic connections (Slack, Drive)

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: Notion Agent, AI Meeting Notes, Enterprise Search, SAML SSO, private teamspaces, premium connections (GitHub, Asana)

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Zero data retention, SCIM, audit log, DLP/SIEM, domain management, CSM, advanced connections

pricing.notes: Custom Agents billed as usage: free to try, then $10 per 1,000 monthly Notion credits. Workers free during beta, start using credits August 11, 2026. Yearly billing saves up to 20%. Some plans may not be available in every workspace.

Frequently Asked Questions
Some questions about Shortcut are asked frequently. We've answered the most frequent of those frequent questions below.
What can I expect each billing cycle if I'm on the yearly Team or Business Plan?
For these plans, we use metered billing, which means we charge you per user, per month during your yearly billing cycle.
We want to cancel our subscription. Can we get a refund?
Shortcut does not offer pro-rated refunds for cancelled subscriptions.
How do I add a credit card in Shortcut?
To add a credit card, go to
Settings > Workspace > Billing
, and update your billing information in the Card section. It’s always a good idea to review and update your billing contact information while you’re there.
What happens when the trial period ends?
If you have 10 or fewer users when the trial ends, you can simply use Shortcut for free or manually choose one of our paid plans to get access to the features included within it. If you have more than 10 users and want to continue using Shortcut after your trial ends, subscribe to one of our paid plans.
Do I have to wait until the end of the trial to subscribe to a paid plan?
Nope! You are welcome to add a credit card at any time, but you won’t be charged until your 14-day Trial is over.
What can I do in Shortcut during the 14-day trial period?
Anything! The Shortcut Trial is meant to give you an opportunity to truly test Shortcut’s features and to build processes that work for your team. You’ll have full access to Shortcut as a robust tool, including (but definitely not limited to): an Organization with unlimited
Workspaces
and
user management
, unlimited
Observer users
,
our API
, and
outgoing webhooks
. Feel free to experiment with using Teams, Epics and Objectives, Stories, Labels, and Custom Workflows together to create a project management environment unique and useful to your team.
For more billing information, check out our
Help Center.
But wait, there’s more....

GitHub Issues
GitHub Issues
Create issues, break them into sub-issues, track progress, add custom fields, and have conversations. Visualize large projects as tables, boards, or roadmaps, and automate everything with code.
Start using projects
Contact sales
Logos for Shopify, Vercel, Stripe, Ford, and NASA
Shopify
Vercel
Stripe
Ford
Nasa
Break issues into sub-issues
Tackle complex issues with sub-issues and track their status with progress indicators. Navigate the full scope of work all in one view.
Streamline conversations
Express ideas with GitHub Flavored Markdown, mention contributors, react with emoji, clarify with attachments, and see references from commits, pull requests, releases, and deploys. Coordinate by assigning contributors and teams, or by adding them to milestones and projects. All in a single timeline.
Upload and attach videos to comments
Dive into work faster with issue forms and templates
Features
Bored of boards?
Switch to tables and roadmaps. Create views for how you work.
Save views for sprints, backlogs, teams, or releases.
Rank, group, sort, slice and filter to suit the occasion. Create swimlanes, share templates and set work in progress limits.
No mouse? No problem.
Every action you can take with the mouse has a keyboard shortcut or command. Filter, sort, group, and assign issues. Your hands never leave the keyboard.
Custom fields
Track metadata like iterations, priority, story points, dates, notes, and links. Add custom fields to projects and edit from the issue sidebar.
Track progress with project insights
Track the health of your current iteration cycle, milestone, or any other custom field you create with new project insights. Identify bottlenecks and issues blocking the team from making progress with the new burn up chart.
Share best practices with project templates
Create templates to share and reuse when getting started with a new project. Share inspiration across teams and get started with a single click.
Manage work automatically
Accelerate your project planning with workflows. Automatically triage issues, set values for custom fields, or archive issues.
Manage work automatically
Issues, where you need them
Issues can be viewed, created, and managed in your browser, your favorite terminal, or on your phone or tablet.
GitHub CLI
View, update, and create issues without ever leaving your terminal.
Learn more
GitHub Mobile
Create and manage issues on the go with our native iOS and Android mobile apps.
Learn more
What developers are saying
“
The new planning and tracking functionality keeps my project management close to my code. I no longer find myself needing to reach for spreadsheets or 3P tools which go stale instantly.
Dan Godfrey
Development Manager
Flexible project planning for developers
Start using projects
Contact sales
Frequently asked questions
What is GitHub Issues?
We all need a way to plan our work, track issues, and discuss the things we build. Our answer to this universal question is GitHub Issues, and it’s built-in to every repository. GitHub’s issue tracking is unique because of our focus on simplicity, references, and elegant formatting.
With GitHub Issues, you can express ideas with GitHub Flavored Markdown, assign and mention contributors, react with emojis, clarify with attachments and videos, plus reference code like commits, pull requests, and deploys. With task lists, you can break big issues into tasks, further organize your work with milestones and labels, and track relationships and dependencies.
We built GitHub Issues for developers. It is simple, adaptable, and powerful.
What are Projects?
As teams and projects grow, how we work evolves. Tools that hard-code a methodology are too specific and rigid to adapt to any moment. Often, we find ourselves creating a spreadsheet or pulling out a notepad to have the space to think. Then our planning is disconnected from where the work happens.
The new Projects connect your planning directly to the work your teams are doing and flexibly adapt to whatever your team needs at any point. Built like a spreadsheet, project tables give you a live canvas to filter, sort, and group issues and pull requests. You can use it, or the accompanying project board, along with custom fields, to track a sprint, plan a feature, or manage a large-scale release.
What plans have access to Projects?
All users have access to the free tier of GitHub Issues and Projects. For more information about paid tiers, see our
pricing page
.
Will the new Projects experience be available in GitHub Enterprise Server?
Yes! GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) support follows our regular cadence of one to two quarters before enabling the on-premises functionality.

Projects
Planning and tracking with Projects
Build adaptable projects to track your work on GitHub.
A project is an adaptable collection of items that you can view as a table, a kanban board, or a roadmap and that stays up-to-date with GitHub data. Your projects can track issues, pull requests, and ideas that you note down.
You can create and customize multiple views by filtering, sorting, and grouping issues and pull requests, visualize work with configurable charts, and add custom fields to track metadata specific to your team. Rather than enforcing a specific methodology, a project provides flexible features you can customize to your team’s needs and processes.

analysis.name: GitHub Projects
analysis.value_proposition: Flexible, code-adjacent project planning built into every GitHub repository, letting developers track issues as tables, boards, or roadmaps.
analysis.ideal_customer: Software development teams already using GitHub for source control who want project management alongside their code

features.name: issues
features.description: Built-in issue tracker for planning work and discussion
features.evidence_quote: Create issues, break them into sub-issues, track progress, add custom fields

features.name: sub-issues
features.description: Break complex issues into nested sub-issues with progress indicators
features.evidence_quote: Tackle complex issues with sub-issues and track their status with progress indicators

features.name: issue dependencies
features.description: Track relationships and blockers between issues
features.evidence_quote: Creating issue dependencies

features.name: issue types
features.description: Managed issue type classifications
features.evidence_quote: Managing issue types

features.name: issue templates
features.description: Issue forms and templates for structured intake
features.evidence_quote: Dive into work faster with issue forms and templates

features.name: table view
features.description: Spreadsheet-style layout for project items
features.evidence_quote: Visualize large projects as tables, boards, or roadmaps

features.name: board view

differentiators: Native to GitHub — project management lives in the same UI/API as code, PRs, commits
Issues, PRs, commits, releases, and deploys auto-cross-reference in a single timeline
Free tier is unusually generous (unlimited public/private repos, Issues & Projects included)
Full keyboard-driven UX for project views
Automatable via GitHub Actions and GraphQL API — code-driven project workflows
Available in browser, CLI, and native mobile apps

praised: 'The new planning and tracking functionality keeps my project management close to my code. I no longer find myself needing to reach for spreadsheets or 3P tools which go stale instantly.' — Dan Godfrey
Flexible, customizable views (table/board/roadmap)
Free and built into every repo — no extra tool to buy
Deep integration with code, PRs, commits
complaints: Perceived as thinner than dedicated PM tools (Jira, Linear) for non-engineering workflows
Insights/reporting less mature than dedicated PM competitors
Limited notification/assignment ergonomics for cross-functional teams
Learning curve for GraphQL-based Projects API

free_tier.limits: Unlimited public/private repos; 2,000 CI/CD minutes/month; 500MB Packages storage; community support

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited repos, Issues & Projects, Dependabot, 2000 Actions min, 500MB Packages

tiers.name: Team
tiers.headline_inclusions: Codespaces access, repo rules, code owners, required/multiple reviewers, draft PRs

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Enterprise platform with SSO, advanced security add-ons, premium support (contact sales)

pricing.notes: Team is $4/user/month for the first 12 months (promotional). Codespaces has separate metered fees ($0.18/hr compute, $0.07/GB/mo storage). Advanced Security and Copilot for Business are paid add-ons.

Capture, organize, and tackle your to-dos from anywhere
Skip to main content
Features
Solutions
Plans
Pricing
Resources
Log in
Get Trello for free
Explore the features that help your team succeed
Inbox
Capture every vital detail from emails, Slack, and more directly into your Trello Inbox.
Planner
Sync your calendar and allocate focused time slots to boost productivity.
Automation
Automate tasks and workflows with Trello.

Templates

Integrations

Meet Trello
Trello makes it easy for your team to get work done. No matter the project, workflow, or type of team, Trello can help keep things organized. It’s simple –  sign-up, create a board, and you’re off! Productivity awaits.
Check out Trello
Take a page out of these pre-built Trello playbooks designed for all teams
Marketing teams
Whether launching a new product, campaign, or creating content, Trello helps marketing teams succeed.
Product management
Use Trello’s management boards and roadmap features to simplify complex projects and processes.
Engineering teams
Ship more code, faster, and give your developers the freedom to be more agile with Trello.
Design teams
Empower your design teams by using Trello to streamline creative requests and promote more fluid cross-team collaboration.
Startups
From hitting revenue goals to managing workflows, small businesses thrive with Trello.
Remote teams
Keep your remote team connected and motivated, no matter where they’re located around the world.
See all teams
Our product in action
Use case: Task management
Track progress of tasks in one convenient place with a visual layout that adds ‘ta-da’ to your to-do’s.
Use case: Resource hub
Save hours when you give teams a well-designed hub to find information easily and quickly.
Use case: Project management
Keep projects organized, deadlines on track, and teammates aligned with Trello.
See all use cases

Trello 101
Stay organized and efficient with Inbox, Boards, and Planner. Every to-do, idea, or responsibility—no matter how small—finds its place, keeping you at the top of your game.
Inbox
When it’s on your mind, it goes in your Inbox. Capture your to-dos from anywhere, anytime.
Boards
Your to-do list may be long, but it can be manageable! Keep tabs on everything from "to-dos to tackle" to "mission accomplished!”
Planner
Drag, drop, get it done. Snap your top tasks into your calendar and make time for what truly matters.
Inbox
When it’s on your mind, it goes in your Inbox. Capture your to-dos from anywhere, anytime.
Boards
Your to-do list may be long, but it can be manageable! Keep tabs on everything from "to-dos to tackle" to "mission accomplished!”
Planner
Drag, drop, get it done. Snap your top tasks into your calendar and make time for what truly matters.
From message to action
Quickly turn communication from your favorite apps into to-dos, keeping all your discussions and tasks organized in one place.
EMAIL MAGIC
Easily turn your emails into to-dos! Just forward them to your Trello Inbox, and they’ll be transformed by AI into organized to-dos with all the links you need.
MESSAGE APP SORCERY
Need to follow up on a message from Slack or Microsoft Teams? Send it directly to your Trello board! Your favorite app interface lets you save messages that appear in your Trello Inbox with AI-generated summaries and links.
WORK SMARTER
Customize the way you organize with easy integrations, automation, and mirroring of your to-dos across multiple locations.
Integrations
Connect the apps you are already using into your Trello workflow or add a Power-Up to fine-tune your specific needs.
Browse Integrations
Automation
No-code automation is built into every Trello board. Focus on the work that matters most and let the robots do the rest.
Get to know Automation
Card mirroring
View all your to-dos from multiple boards in one place. Mirror a card to keep track of work wherever you need it!
Compare plans

Privacy Policy
Terms
Copyright © 2024 Atlassian
Manage Preferences
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
More information
Accept all
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Targeting Cookies
Always Active
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.
Functional Cookies
Always Active
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Performance Cookies
Always Active
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

Manage Preferences
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
More information
Accept all
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Targeting Cookies
Always Active
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.
Functional Cookies
Always Active
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Performance Cookies
Always Active
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

integrations.text:
Power-Up Your Productivity with Trello Integrations
Skip to main content
Features
Solutions
Plans

Resources

Explore the features that help your team succeed
Inbox

Automation

Meet Trello
Trello makes it easy for your team to get work done. No matter the project, workflow, or type of team, Trello can help keep things organized. It’s simple –  sign-up, create a board, and you’re off! Productivity awaits.
Check out Trello
Take a page out of these pre-built Trello playbooks designed for all teams
Marketing teams
Whether launching a new product, campaign, or creating content, Trello helps marketing teams succeed.
Product management
Use Trello’s management boards and roadmap features to simplify complex projects and processes.
Engineering teams
Ship more code, faster, and give your developers the freedom to be more agile with Trello.
Design teams
Empower your design teams by using Trello to streamline creative requests and promote more fluid cross-team collaboration.
Startups
From hitting revenue goals to managing workflows, small businesses thrive with Trello.
Remote teams
Keep your remote team connected and motivated, no matter where they’re located around the world.
See all teams

analysis.name: Trello
analysis.tagline: Capture, organize, and tackle your to-dos from anywhere
analysis.value_proposition: A visual, board-based work management tool that helps individuals and teams organize tasks, projects, and workflows using cards, lists, and boards.
analysis.ideal_customer: Small-to-mid teams (up to 100 on Premium) and individuals needing lightweight visual task/project management — marketing, product, engineering, design, startups, remote teams

features.name: inbox
features.description: Central capture surface for pulling in tasks from emails, Slack, and other sources
features.evidence_quote: Capture every vital detail from emails, Slack, and more directly into your Trello Inbox.

features.name: planner
features.description: Calendar sync with focused time-block allocation for tasks
features.evidence_quote: Sync your calendar and allocate focused time slots to boost productivity.

features.name: automation
features.description: Rule-based automation of tasks and workflows (Butler)
features.evidence_quote: Automate tasks and workflows with Trello.

features.name: power-ups
features.description: Third-party plugins that extend board functionality with connected tools
features.evidence_quote: Power up your teams by linking their favorite tools with Trello plugins.

features.name: templates
features.description: Pre-built board templates from Trello community and industry leaders
features.evidence_quote: Give your team a blueprint for success with easy-to-use templates from industry leaders and the Trello community.

features.name: integrations
features.description: Native connectors to popular third-party productivity apps
features.evidence_quote: Find the apps your team is already using or discover new ways to get work done in Trello.

features.name: boards
features.description: Kanban-style visual boards for organizing work items
features.evidence_quote: sign-up, create a board, and you're off!

features.name: kanban view
features.description: Card-and-list visual layout for task tracking
features.evidence_quote: Track progress of tasks in one convenient place with a visual layout

features.name: roadmap view
features.description: Timeline/roadmap view for planning multi-step projects
features.evidence_quote: Use Trello's management boards and roadmap features to simplify complex projects

features.name: multiple views
features.description: Alternative visualizations of work beyond the default board (calendar, timeline, etc.)
features.evidence_quote: need to track multiple projects and visualize work in a variety of ways.

features.name: task management
features.description: Visual to-do tracking with progress indicators
features.evidence_quote: Track progress of tasks in one convenient place with a visual layout that adds 'ta-da' to your to-do's.

features.name: project management
features.description: Keep projects organized with deadlines and team alignment
features.evidence_quote: Keep projects organized, deadlines on track, and teammates aligned with Trello.

features.name: resource hub
features.description: Shared hub layout for teams to find information
features.evidence_quote: Save hours when you give teams a well-designed hub to find information easily and quickly.

features.name: slack integration
features.description: Capture messages from Slack into Trello Inbox
features.evidence_quote: Capture every vital detail from emails, Slack, and more directly into your Trello Inbox.

features.name: email integration
features.description: Turn emails into Trello cards via Inbox
features.evidence_quote: Capture every vital detail from emails, Slack, and more directly

features.name: calendar sync
features.description: Two-way calendar synchronization with Planner
features.evidence_quote: Sync your calendar and allocate focused time slots

features.name: developer api
features.description: Public API for building Power-Ups and custom integrations
features.evidence_quote: The sky's the limit in what you can deliver to Trello users in your Power-Up!

features.name: admin controls
features.description: Enterprise-grade admin and management tools
features.evidence_quote: Everything your enterprise teams and admins need to manage projects.

features.name: team collaboration
features.description: Multi-user boards for collaborative workflows
features.evidence_quote: For teams that need to manage more work and scale collaboration.

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native mobile apps for iOS and Android
features.evidence_quote: tackle your to-dos from anywhere

features.name: use case: marketing
features.description: Purpose-built playbook for marketing team campaigns and launches
features.evidence_quote: Whether launching a new product, campaign, or creating content, Trello helps marketing teams succeed.

features.name: use case: engineering
features.description: Agile-style workflow support for engineering teams
features.evidence_quote: Ship more code, faster, and give your developers the freedom to be more agile with Trello.

features.name: use case: design
features.description: Creative-request intake and cross-team collaboration
features.evidence_quote: streamline creative requests and promote more fluid cross-team collaboration.

differentiators: Simple kanban-first UX ('sign-up, create a board, and you're off')
Flexible pricing that scales from individual to 2,000-person teams ('you only pay for what you need')
Extensible via Power-Ups plugin ecosystem
Templates from industry leaders + community
Broad team-type applicability (marketing, product, engineering, design, startups, remote)

praised: Simple, visual kanban approach — popularized the format
Extensibility via Power-Ups
complaints: Account/ownership handling — 'Trello handed over my personal account to my previous company' (HN, 731 pts, 260 comments)
Performance issues at scale — engineering team publicly wrote about 'making Trello boards load fast' (HN, 445 pts)
Users building their own PM tools 'after trying Trello, Asana, ClickUp' (HN, 632 pts) — suggests feature gaps
Self-hosted alternatives (Focalboard, Kan.bn) gaining traction as Trello replacements

free_tier.limits: For individuals or small teams looking to keep work organized

tiers.name: Free

landing.text:
Basecamp

Reliable to the core
— A multi-decade track record
The refreshingly straightforward project management system that’s rock-solid and easy to use.
Take a 3 minute tour of Basecamp
Simply put, we get more work done, quicker, and better.
Productivity is up. Errors are down. Clients are happier.
Patrick Sheffield, Moore Communications Group
Information flows like water. A lot more transparency. Everyone is on the same page.
No more secrets and blindspots.
Aaron Bingaman, Penn State Office of Emergency Management
Since using Basecamp, our
communication is drastically better and deadlines are met without drama.
Shannon Kropf, Full Sail University
I no longer have to wonder whether I’ve asked for something...
Every member of my team is more accountable.
John Drover, Roebothan McKay Marshall
We’re much better organised
and it’s way easier to deal with clients and projects.
Pedro Lopes, Coimbra Genomics
Basecamp make us tighter as a group.
Basecamp makes it easy to create shared understanding in our company.
Teddy Zetterlund, Lägenhetsbyte
We don’t have to contact each other about every little thing.
When we need to know something, it’s right there.
Kelly Hunter, Chamber Nation
We used to wonder what everyone was doing.
Now we move forward gracefully.
John McCulley, Moxley Carmichael
It eliminated the need for a lot of extra communication and
reduced a lot of miscommunication between my team.
Anthony Clark, Clark Partners Realty Group
Action items never get lost in the shuffle anymore.
New ideas and projects actually get off the ground now.
Andrew Tyne, MPI Atlantic Canada Chapter
We are more organized. And because of this
we can turn around projects much more quickly now than before.
Kevin Duffy, TRUE Marketing
Check out over 1,000 more customer testimonials…
Tell me if this sounds about right.
Hey there—
There’s a good chance we know why you’re here.
You’re juggling people, projects, and expectations. There are tasks to do, discussions to have, decisions to make, files to share, deadlines to hit, relationships to manage, and work to deliver.
This all has to happen
somewhere
. But you’ve discovered that spreading everything across different apps, browser tabs, emails, and chats doesn’t work. It’s finally time to find a single system.
Unfortunately, most project management systems are bloated, complicated, and confusing. And software that’s hard to use doesn’t get used.
Over 20 years ago we were in the same boat. We needed something capable, but it had to be straightforward and easy. Nothing fit the bill. So we invented Basecamp, and we've been steadily improving it ever since. In software, longevity like this isn't luck — it's proof it works.
And BC5 is all new for 2026
.
Today, Basecamp is used across every industry, and nearly a million people, teams, companies, and non-profits worldwide
rely on it daily
.
Thanks for checking us out. We invite you to give Basecamp a try. And ultimately, we’d be honored to have you as a customer.
Jason Fried,
jason@basecamp.com
Co-founder & CEO
Big numbers. Highly-trusted. Rugged, reliable, and ready.
Over
84 million people
have Basecamp accounts.
Customers have managed more than
60 million projects
.
Over
1.7 billion to-dos
have been added.
Over
350 million message board posts
have been published.
Over
2.7 billion comments
have been posted.
More than
2.2 billion files
have been shared.
More than
5 petabytes
of data is stored by Basecamp.
And a
99.99% uptime record
that spans decades.
Remember when companies cared about service? We still do.
When’s the last time you praised a tech company’s customer service?
Our customers do it all the time
.
Service isn’t an afterthought, it’s one of Basecamp’s best features.
And get this: On average, each member of our customer service team has been with us 9 years. That’s dedication.
Meet Chase, our Head of Support
A stable, well-run business is part of the deal, too.
We’ve been running a business focused on the fundamentals for nearly three decades.
You can’t build reliable software unless you build a reliable company.
Founded in 1999, we’ve been profitable for 27 years in a row.
Zero debt, privately held, and built to stay, not exit.
99.99% historical
uptime
, with full transparency.
Cancel anytime, take your data with you, no lock in.
Direct access to our
CEO via email
.
All these questions have the same answer: Yes!
Can I
hide unfinished work from clients?
Can I
link up Google Docs, Figma, Dropbox, etc?
Can I
see all my tasks instantly anytime?
Can I
see everything that’s overdue on one screen?
Can I use Basecamp
if some teammates prefer email?
Can I
see all the work that was completed in a day?
Can I
set up projects that only some can see?
Can I
see a full history of someone’s work?
Can I
easily see someone else’s assignments?
Can I
see every deadline on a single calendar?
Can I
easily reference an entire conversation from last year?
Can I
follow a project without receiving notifications?
Can I
assign tasks to multiple people at once?
Can I
see all our projects laid out on a visual timeline?
Can I
follow-up with someone without anyone else knowing?
Can
our clients respond to our questions via email?
Can
our clients see a record of everything we’ve asked them?
Can
client feeback be documented for the record?
Can I
organize ideas and setup workflows on a Kanban board?
Can Basecamp
tell me when someone finished something?
Can Basecamp
help me get a client to approve a design?
Can Basecamp
keep my team accountable?
Check out the
full feature list
for more.
Join us for a free live class.
Curious about Basecamp but want to see it live and ask some questions first? Join us for a free live class.
We’ll show you how to set it up in minutes, how to roll it out to your team, and take Q&A covering anything else you’d like to ask.
See all upcoming classes
.

pricing.title: Basecamp — Pricing
pricing.text:
Basecamp — Pricing
We’d like to use cookies to help understand if our ads are working or not.
No, thanks
Yes, that’s fine
Pick a package and try Basecamp for free.
2,707
organizations signed up last week. You’re in good company.
Basecamp is beautiful software that has resisted every wrong trend and stayed true to the things that mattered most. Highly recommended.
Tobi Lutke
Shopify Co-Founder & CEO
Want a walkthrough? Live Q&A session?
Book a personal demo
with our team.
Pro Unlimited
Top-of-the-line, all-inclusive pricing. Unlimited users, no per-user fees.
$299/month, billed annually
Your whole organization for one fixed price.
Unlimited users, plus every feature & upgrade.
Try it free for 60 days
Unlimited projects
5 terabytes
storage space (10x Pro)
Fixed price — no per-user charges
Includes
Admin Pro Pack upgrade
?
Gives account owners and admins greater control over access, permissions, and more
Includes
Timesheet upgrade
?
Track hours/time spent working on projects
Personal onboarding
with our team
?
One of our experts is happy to walk your team through Basecamp to get you up to speed
Priority
24/7/365 customer support, your questions get answered first
?
Jump to the front of the queue when you contact support
More payment options
including credit card, check, ACH, or wire
Extended
60-day free trial
Pro
A great choice for freelancers, startups, and smaller teams.
$15/user, billed monthly
Invite guests and clients for free. We will only bill you for employees.
Try it free for 30 days
Unlimited projects
500 GB storage space
Pay-per-user
Optional upgrades sold separately
Self-guided onboarding
24/7/365 customer support
Pay by credit card
30-day free trial
Or, just
run 1 project free
with 1 GB of storage and up to 20 users. If you need more projects/storage you can upgrade later.
Sign up
The same core features are included with every package.
From Free to Pro Unlimited, you get
all the essential fundamentals built in.
Message Boards
for internal announcements
To-dos
for tracking work and accountability
Card Tables
is our take on Kanban for process tracking
Campfires & Pings
for group chat and direct messaging
Scheduling
for deadlines, milestones, and events
Docs & Files
for storing assets
Reports & Automatic Check-ins
for staying up on things
A stable, well-run company is part of the deal, too.
We’ve been running an upstanding, trustworthy, sustainable business for nearly three decades.
You can’t build reliable software unless you build a reliable company.
27 profitable, financially responsible years in business
Zero debt, privately held, and built to stay, not exit
99.99% historical uptime, with full transparency
Gold standard, famously great customer service
Extensive learning library free for all customers
Direct access to our CEO via email
Risk-free, cancel anytime, no long-term lock-in
With Basecamp, cancellation is entirely self-serve, no questions asked, no retention specialists trying to talk you out of it. Cancel any time, no long-term contracts to lock you in. Simple, straightforward, and fair, just as it should be.
A big boost for bootstrappers & small businesses
We started our business without outside funding, and you probably did too. We know your money’s precious, and we want to help. That’s why Basecamp is intentionally affordable for start-ups, freelancers, and small businesses just getting started.
Basecamp eliminates the Hassle Tax
There’s no more paying separate bills, onboarding people in multiple places, training people how to use different tools, managing expenses across vendors, etc… the benefits of Basecamp go beyond price.
I have pricing questions…
Can we upgrade, downgrade, switch packages, or cancel whenever we want?
Yes, you can upgrade
from Free to Plus or from Plus to Pro Unlimited whenever you’d like.
Yes, you can downgrade
from Pro Unlimited to Plus or Free as long as your usage fits on the lower package. For example, if you still had five projects, you couldn’t downgrade to the Free package which only allows one project. You’d have to delete four of those projects before downgrading.
Yes, you can cancel any time
, no questions asked, no forms to fill out, no person to talk to. If you cancel an annual package, the difference will be prorated back to your credit card.
Do you offer non-profit or educational discounts?
Absolutely.
Basecamp is 10% off for registered 501(c)(3) non-profits, and 100% free for K-12, homeschoolers, or Universities as long as it’s used for class work only.
For non-profits:
Email us
a copy of your 501(c)(3) paperwork right after you sign up. We’ll apply the discount once we verify your status.
For teachers and homeschool parents:
Right after you sign up,
email us
from your school account with the name of your school, the class you teach, and proof you’re a teacher. We’ll apply the discount after we verify everything.
For students:
Right after you sign up,
email us
from your school account with the name of your school, what you’re studying, and when you expect to graduate. We’ll apply the discount after we verify everything.
Note:
We can only discount one account. If you need to support multiple groups, one account will give you everything you need to support them. If you’ve been paying for Basecamp, we can’t refund or discount payments made before your discount started. We reserve the right to end the discount program due to chronic abuse of the free accounts or other circumstances. If we do, anyone who is already participating will continue to have their discounts applied. We just won’t offer up new free or discounted accounts.
Could we really add 1000 users and still just pay $299/month total on Pro Unlimited?
Yes.
Basecamp is one of the few products in the entire industry with an all-inclusive, unlimited users package for one fixed, capped price. We pioneered this all-in package, and have been offering it for over a decade.
If we have clients, or work with contractors, do we have to pay for them too?
No.
Clients and contractors can be added to your account for free. On the Plus package, we only bill you $15/user/month for employees or full users, not clients or temporary contractors.
Do I need a credit card to try Basecamp?
Nope.
We don’t require a credit card on the Free account, or for any of the free trials. If you choose a pay package with a free trial, you’ll be asked to enter your credit card at the end of your trial if you want to continue. It’s entirely up to you.
Will I automatically be charged when my 30 or 60-day free trial is up?
No.
We don’t ask for a credit card to try Basecamp, so we couldn’t charge you even if we wanted to. If you want to continue once your trial is up, then you’d enter your credit card. You’re in complete control.
How do upgrades work? What’s included with Timesheet or the Admin Pro Pack?
While Timesheet and Admin Pro Pack are included on the Pro Unlimited package, customers on the Plus package can optionally upgrade to either or both whenever they’d like.
Timesheet
allows you to track time spent on tasks, writing, meetings, or other pieces of work within a Basecamp project. You can then run reports to aggregate time spent across projects, or per-person, to help with resource allocation or client billing. You can also export the data to bring into an accounting or billing system of your choice. The Timesheet upgrade is $50/month flat no matter how many people you have on your account.
this video shows you how it works
.
Admin Pro Pack
gives account owners and admins greater control over access and permissions and allows them to:
Change who can send Pings (per company)
Change who can edit project details
Change who can edit people on a project
Change who can move, archive, and delete content
Change who can turn on public links
Limit editing and deleting comments & chat lines to 15 minutes after posting
Require two-factor authentication
Change chat history settings
Set Out of Office for others
The Admin Pro Pack upgrade is $50/month flat no matter how many people you have on your account. You can
learn more about Admin Pro Pack here
.
Can we buy more storage if we need it? How much is it?
Yes.
You can add an additional 1 terabyte (that’s 1000 gigabytes) of storage for $50/month flat. The storage can be used by all users across your entire account.
On the free one project package, can I delete the project and start another one?
Yes.
You can only run one project at time on the Free package, but if you complete a project, or just want to get rid of the one you’re currently running, you can delete it and free up the slot to start a new one. You can’t, however, keep more than one around. So in order to start a new one, you must delete the current one.
And there’s more…

analysis.name: Basecamp
analysis.tagline: The refreshingly straightforward project management system that's rock-solid and easy to use.
analysis.value_proposition: An all-in-one project management platform that consolidates tasks, communication, files, and scheduling into one simple system with flat-rate unlimited-user pricing.
analysis.ideal_customer: Small to mid-sized teams and client-services agencies that want to consolidate tools without per-seat pricing pressure and value simplicity over feature depth

Non-profits get 10% off; K-12 and universities free for classwork. 5 TB storage on Pro Unlimited is described as '10x Pro'.

spec.body:
# Zergboard

Lean multi-tenant project management app (Trello/Linear inspired). **The most mature ZergStack microproduct.** Already has 4 pricing tiers and is in active internal use across Zerg's marketing, ops, product, and client work.

## Positioning

**One-liner:** Project management built for AI agents to share with humans, not bolt onto.

**ICP:** Small-to-mid engineering, ops, and GTM teams (5–50 seats) running AI agents in daily workflows: Claude, GPT, or custom automation for task management, connector tracking, or recurring operational programs. Secondary: Jira/ClickUp refugees at growing teams.

**Hook:** Scoped API tokens plus tenant-safe routes mean agents read and update cards at scale without breaking blast radius.

### ZergStack pillars (shared across family)

- **AI-native:** agents are first-class participants, not bolted-on bots, webhooks, or sidebar widgets
- **ZergStack-interconnected:** shared auth, agents, and data primitives. Adopting one product lowers the cost of adopting the next
- **Much cheaper:** undercuts category leaders on price by design, not as a temporary promo
- **Easy to automate:** agent context flows across products without webhook scaffolding

### Zergboard-specific

- Built agent-native from day one. Linear and Trello are adding agent surfaces; Zergboard shipped with them
- Webhooks ship in the standard tier, not gated behind enterprise
- Already running real production work: 60+ cards on Andesite connector tracking, 32 on CesiumAstro, plus Zerg's own marketing program

_Source: [[Competitive/pm-software/positioning|pm-software positioning brief]] (2026-05-03; revise as needed)._

## What It Is

A kanban + task management tool with first-class agent support. Tenant-safe API routes designed for agentic flows (the zergboard-skill calls into it via API tokens).

## Features

- Email/password auth with secure session cookies
- Scoped API-token auth for CLI / automation
- Multi-tenant orgs + user memberships
- Boards and columns; closable board status
- Kanban cards with labels, comments, subtasks, dependencies, drag/drop
- Tenant-safe API routes for agent flows
- Fly.io deployment-ready (deployed as `zergboard` in `lax`, external Postgres)

## Internal Usage (Active Boards)

Pulled 2026-04-30 from the Zerg workspace and Matt's personal workspace. See [[Epoch/Projects/Zergboard Snapshot|Zergboard Snapshot]] for full board pull.

| Board | Prefix | Org | Active Cards |
|-------|--------|-----|--------------|
| Marketing | MAR | Zerg | 11 |
| Website | WEB | Zerg | (created 2026-05-01) |
| Ops | OPS | Zerg | 5 |
| CesiumAstro | CES | Zerg | 32 |
| DCAIG Status | DCA | Zerg | 60+ (Andesite connector tracking) |
| zergboard | ZER | Zerg | 2 (internal bug tracker) |
| Matthew's Zergboard | CARD | Personal | 8 |

**Marketing program** runs across two boards: **Website** (id `8ef863c1-765f-493e-8622-2e65b4d2ca61`) for site-build/conversion work; **Marketing** (id `7bf7ab2a-ac70-4b29-85bf-74a6db6a0760`) for the recurring program (content, launches, channels, measurement). All cards on either board carry lane prefixes in titles: `[Content]`, `[Launches]`, `[Channels]`, `[Brand & Site]`, `[Pipeline]`, `[Measurement]`, `[Infra]`, `[Tools]`.

## Open Product Bugs (ZER board)

- **ZER-1** — Add MFA
- **ZER-2** — Users not being added to workspace/board
- **UUID `bdb9aa66-482d-4ce8-ab23-733ed0225e97`** — Card external-id resolution can mutate the wrong board when prefixes collide. Do not reference this by external id because the duplicated `ZER-11` id is the bug. Local Claude/Codex skill wrappers now fail closed; product-side org-unique external-id fix is implemented locally on branch `fix/zergboard-external-id-ambiguity` in `/private/tmp/zerg-zstack-zergboard`; local typecheck/build pass, migration notes are added, and remaining work is staging migration verification, merge/deploy, and live verification.
- **ZER-12** — Remove legacy email ingest body secret after Bearer migration. Filed as follow-up from the external-id fix QA; target no earlier than 2026-06-10 after 14 clean days with no request-body secret usage in logs.
- **ZER-13** — Post-merge hardening for org-scoped external IDs: live Postgres integration test, keyboard shortcut/help cleanup, import scaling, board guest assignee picker, and duplicate-ID migration audit mapping.

## CLI / Skill

The `zergboard-skill` (in `~/.claude/skills/zergboard-skill/`, mirrored to `~/.codex/skills/zergboard-skill/`) lets agents read/manage cards, boards, and workspaces via the API. Quirks worth remembering:

- Use full UUIDs for destructive actions; the local wrappers reject ambiguous external ids and board names
- `status` is not the same as a column; they're separate fields
- Multiline descriptions need a Python subprocess wrapper to pass cleanly

## Local Build Notes

- 2026-05-11: restored `zergboard/server/lib/*` from commit `4435416e` and unignored `zergboard/server/lib/**` in `.gitignore`; `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` pass in `/Users/mattheweisner/zerg/zergboard`.
- 2026-05-11: product-side external-id fix lives in `/private/tmp/zerg-zstack-zergboard` on branch `fix/zergboard-external-id-ambiguity` from `origin/zstack/zergboard`; `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` pass there. Fakeidan's latest successful formal pass was `/private/tmp/qa-gate-artifacts/20260511-181231-490775-8f5e1597/manifest.json`; later usable findings from malformed artifact `/private/tmp/qa-gate-artifacts/20260511-214945-494683-f69eebeb/qa-gate-artifact-bundle.fakeidan-code.2026-05-11.md` were patched too. Final focused fakeidan reruns timed out at the Claude CLI layer, so needs staging migration verification plus final re-review when healthy or explicit waiver before merge/deploy.

## Documentation (in repo)

- Static docs page: `/docs`
- Index: `~/zerg/zergboard/docs/README.md`
- Architecture: `~/zerg/zergboard/docs/architecture.md`
- API reference: `~/zerg/zergboard/docs/api.md`

## Pricing

**Locked 2026-05-05** (per ZergStack-wide decision): Free / **$1 Basic** / **$9 Pro** / Custom Enterprise. Implementation in PR #283 against `zergboard/pages/index.vue` (replaces prior $12/$39/$149 Core/Growth/Scale tier shape). Bundle SKUs ($4 Basic, $19 Pro for the full Zstack) tracked in [[Writing/Zstack Microproduct Pricing (WEB-31)]]. Strategy: `project_zstack_pricing.md`.

## Competitive Context

- Review: [[Competitive/pm-software/index|pm-software]] (2026-05-02)
- 29 table-stakes gaps; 3 whitespace bets (sub-issues, ai triage, triage rules); 11 spec↔live drift items
- Positioning brief: [[Competitive/pm-software/positioning|pm-software/positioning]] — "Project management built for AI agents"
- Deep-dive candidate: see MAR-28 on Zergboard

## Related

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Zstack|ZergStack]] — umbrella
- [[Epoch/Projects/Zergboard Snapshot|Zergboard Snapshot]] — recurring board pull
- [[Epoch/Projects/Linear Snapshot|Linear Snapshot]] — engineering-side ticket tracking (separate)

built for teams running AI agents
Trello-simple kanban with Linear-level structure, $1/seat — and the only PM tool where AI agents are first-class teammates with their own scoped tokens and tenant-safe routes.

7 tickets
from QA failures in the last 15 minutes.
Docs
Full product, API, integration, deployment, and CLI docs are available as static pages.
Open docs
Everything a focused PM tool needs
A compact kanban surface plus the plumbing you need for real teams and automation.
Cards and columns
Real-time kanban with fast drag-and-drop. Keyboard-first; agents drive it through the same primitives.
Subtasks and dependencies
Break work down without spinning up a sprint tool. Block one card on another. On every paid tier — including $1/seat Basic.
Labels and comments
Card labels, threaded comments, mentions. Free tier included.
Scoped API tokens
Per-agent identity with tenant-safe routes. Bound an agent to one workspace, one board, one role — so a misbehaving prompt can't reach data it shouldn't see. The reason teams move from Basic to Pro.
Multi-tenant workspaces
Organizations, memberships, and isolated data out of the box.
API-first architecture
Drive workflows via API for automations, bots, and agents.
Admin controls
Superuser panel for platform-level metrics and governance.
Webhooks included
Every card and board event, on every paid tier. No enterprise upcharge.
Every primitive a real team needs. $1/seat.
Trello hides subtasks behind Power-Ups. Asana gates dependencies to Business. Linear charges $8/seat for what we ship at $1. Real work shouldn't need three upgrades.
Labels and statuses
Comments and threads
Subtasks and dependencies
Closable boards
Audit exports
Admin panel for platform-level metrics and governance
How agents work in Zergboard
Agents are first-class teammates here, not bolted-on bots.
Triage incoming work
A scoped agent reads new cards, applies labels, sets priority, and routes to the right column — with a comment explaining its reasoning.
Resolve QA failures
When QA finds a regression, an agent files the card with the right tags and assigns to the on-call engineer.
Cross-board context
With multi-board scope, an agent on Zergboard can pull project data from Zerg's Marketing board to inform a sprint card — something Linear and Jira can't do across tenants.
Why $1 isn't a typo
Linear, Asana, and ClickUp built their own infrastructure stacks independently. We built ours on ZTC, the runtime that powers every Zerg product. One stack across five products means the unit cost of running Zergboard is a fraction of what it costs Linear to run Linear.
AI-native operations make the difference even larger. Support, onboarding, and a meaningful chunk of platform ops are agent-driven instead of headcount-driven. We pass the savings on.
The catch (if you want to call it that): every Zerg product runs on the same shared identity, agents, and data primitives. ZergMeeting drops cards onto Zergboard. ZergChat threads link to them. ZergCal schedules from them. Adopting one product makes adopting the next nearly free.
Three tiers. No contact-sales wall.
Start free, switch from Linear or Asana to Basic at $1/seat, upgrade to Pro at $9/seat when you need agent governance.

Frequently asked questions
Can agents use Zergboard directly?
▸
Yes. The API is intentionally minimal, stable, and built for automation-driven ticket flows.
Do you support multiple clients and tenants?
▸
Yes. Each organization is isolated with membership-based access control.
Can I self-host later?
▸
Yes. The stack is Nuxt + Postgres with portable deployment primitives.
Is there a free tier?
▸
Yes. Start free for up to 3 users and upgrade only when your team grows.
