positioning.elevator_pitch: Zergboard is agent-native project management for 5–50-seat teams running Claude, GPT, or custom automations daily — built with scoped API tokens and tenant-safe routes from day one, not bolted on like Jira or Linear.
core_differentiator_lines: Agent-native from day one, not a 2026 retrofit.
Scoped API tokens keep agent blast radius tenant-safe.
Webhooks in the standard tier, not enterprise-gated.
Audit log built in — see every agent and human action.
Priced to undercut ClickUp and Jira by design.

headline_candidates.headline: Project management built for agents to share with humans.
headline_candidates.subhead: Scoped API tokens and tenant-safe routes let Claude and GPT read and write cards at scale without breaking blast radius.

headline_candidates.headline: Your agents already work here. Stop bolting them on.
headline_candidates.subhead: Linear and Trello are retrofitting agent surfaces in 2026. Zergboard shipped with them.

headline_candidates.headline: The lightweight PM tool Jira refugees keep asking for.
headline_candidates.subhead: No process bloat. No enterprise-gated webhooks. Real production work across 100+ active cards.

headline_candidates.headline: Every card change is auditable — human or agent.
headline_candidates.subhead: Full audit log ships in the standard tier. Know exactly what your automations touched, when, and why.

headline_candidates.headline: PM software that gets cheaper as your agent count grows.
headline_candidates.subhead: Seat-based pricing that undercuts the category, and scoped API tokens instead of per-agent seat charges.

messaging_pillars.promise: Your agents are first-class participants — same auth model, same permissions, same audit trail as humans.
proof_points: Scoped API tokens with tenant-safe route enforcement
Zergboard-skill drives 60+ cards on Andesite connector tracking via API
Shipped agent-first in 2026, not retrofit like Linear/Trello/Monday

messaging_pillars.promise: Every card mutation — human or automated — is logged with actor, scope, and payload.
proof_points: Full audit log in standard tier (Jira gates this to enterprise)
Answers 'what did my agent just do at 3am' without SIEM stitching
Reduces blast radius fear that blocks agent rollouts

messaging_pillars.pillar: Lean by design, not by omission
messaging_pillars.promise: The Trello/Linear feel without the ClickUp surface-area sprawl or Jira workflow theology.
proof_points: Kanban + subtasks + dependencies + comments — no 47-view configurator
In active production across 5 internal Zerg boards (100+ cards)
Same primitives across ZergStack — one auth, one data model

messaging_pillars.promise: Undercuts Jira, ClickUp, and Monday.com structurally — not as a limited promo.
proof_points: Webhooks + API tokens in standard tier (competitors gate these)
Adopting one ZergStack product lowers cost of adopting the next

anti_pitch: We are NOT another ClickUp — no 200-feature grab bag chasing every persona. HN thread 'How can a product like ClickUp grow so fast' shows the market is skeptical of surface-area sprawl.
We are NOT Jira — no workflow theology, no admin priesthood, no 'JIRA is an antipattern' vibes (HN 1012↑).
We are NOT Notion — your data is exportable via API from day one. Notion's 'withholding my company data' thread (479↑) is the anti-pattern.
We are NOT Monday.com — no ambiguous 'is it better than the others?' identity crisis (HN Ask HN thread). We have one clear ICP: teams running agents.
We do NOT compete on visual polish or template marketplaces — those are anchors for the wrong ICP.
positioning.icp_refinement: 5–50-seat engineering, ops, and GTM teams already running Claude/GPT/custom agents in daily workflows — where 'my agent needs to update 60 cards without a human in the loop' is a real Tuesday problem. Sharpest wedge: technical founders and eng leads who've felt the blast-radius fear of pointing an LLM at a PM tool with global API keys. Secondary: Jira/ClickUp refugees at 10–30-person teams who want lightweight kanban without workflow ceremony, and who've read the HN threads about Plane/Tara/Focalboard but want something maintained.

objection_handling.objection: Linear/Trello are adding agent features too — why switch?
objection_handling.response: They're bolting agents onto human-first data models. Zergboard's scoped tokens and tenant-safe routes were designed for agent traffic first — the difference shows up the moment you try to give an agent write access at scale without a security review.

That's the complaint, not the pitch — HN threads on ClickUp bloat and 'making a ClickUp/Asana alternative' show the market wants less surface area. We ship the primitives (kanban, subtasks, dependencies, comments, audit log) that 90% of teams actually use.

Jira's own users call it 'an antipattern' (HN 1012↑) and 'I fucking hate Jira' (HN 971↑). We're for the teams leaving, not the teams stuck. And unlike Jira, we don't gate webhooks and audit logs behind enterprise tiers.

objection_handling.objection: You're missing custom fields, saved views, dark mode, mobile apps, Slack integration.
objection_handling.response: Fair — those are on the near-term roadmap and we're closing the table-stakes gap. What we won't chase: the 40 features past table-stakes that make ClickUp feel like a settings menu with a product attached. Our wedge is agent-native + audit log, and that ships today.

objection_handling.objection: Zerg is a small team — what if you disappear?
objection_handling.response: Zergboard is already the most mature ZergStack product with 4 pricing tiers, 100+ active cards across internal Zerg boards, and paying-adjacent client usage (Andesite, CesiumAstro). And the API-first design means your data is exportable — unlike Notion, where users have posted about being locked out (HN 479↑).

objection_handling.objection: Why not just self-host Plane or Focalboard?
objection_handling.response: You can — and if 'free + I run the ops' beats 'agent-native + managed', that's the right call. Our pitch is for teams whose agent workload is already a bottleneck and who'd rather pay per seat than staff Postgres.

differentiation_opportunities.name: Multi-Agent Role Scoping (Per-Agent-Identity Blast-Radius ACL)
differentiation_opportunities.what: Each agent identity gets a declarative ACL — read/write specific boards, columns, and card fields — enforced server-side per request, not at the API-key level
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Teams running multiple specialized agents (triage agent, standup agent, PM agent) need isolation so one misconfigured agent cannot corrupt all boards; no competitor offers per-agent ACL without enterprise SSO tier
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires rearchitecting auth around agent identities rather than human-proxy tokens — Jira/Linear/Asana treat all API tokens as human-equivalent with no agent-aware permission graph; rebuilding that is 12-18 months minimum against their existing auth models
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Linear and Asana expose single-scope API tokens with no agent-identity model; Jira's 'antipattern' criticism stems partly from inability to safely delegate authority to automated actors at granular scope

differentiation_opportunities.name: Zstack Financial Context on Cards (Zergwallet Bridge)
differentiation_opportunities.what: Cards optionally surface live budget/burn data from Zergwallet — linked budget envelope, current spend, and variance — editable by agents or humans, visible inline on the card without a webhook
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: AI-native teams managing project and budget in two disconnected tools is the next friction point; no PM tool has native treasury context because none built a financial product in the same auth/agent layer
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires a live, shared data primitive across a PM tool and a financial product in the same agent layer — Linear cannot ship this without acquiring a fintech product; Asana/Monday partner integrations are webhook-based and stale by design
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: No competitor in the scanned set has live financial context on cards; Monday.com's 'budget' column is a manual number field, not a live ledger — Monday was called 'terrible for PMs' partly because of this kind of cosmetic feature depth

differentiation_opportunities.name: Agent Triage Rules Engine (Agent-invoked, Not Webhook-fired)
differentiation_opportunities.what: Declarative rules like 'when a card enters Blocked, invoke the triage agent with board history + card context + Zstack memory' — agent reasons about the block and posts a structured resolution suggestion as a card comment
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Current whitespace includes 'ai triage' and 'triage rules'; ClickUp/Asana automation rules fire webhooks to HTTP endpoints, not agents with context — the generational shift to AI agents makes rule-invoked agents 10x more useful than rule-invoked HTTP calls
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: ClickUp/Asana rules engines are webhook dispatchers; wiring in a stateful agent with board memory and Zstack context requires rebuilding their automation layer around an agent runtime — not a feature addition but a platform rewrite for incumbents
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Whitespace note explicitly calls out 'ai triage, triage rules'; ClickUp's automation complexity is already a community complaint — adding agent-invoked rules on top of their existing UI debt is a non-starter for their team

differentiation_opportunities.name: Agent-authored Card Reasoning Audit Trail
differentiation_opportunities.what: Every agent action on a card (status change, comment, dependency link) appends a structured reasoning block: 'Agent moved this to Done because: subtasks X and Y resolved; PR #42 merged; no open blockers' — persisted in card history alongside human actions
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Enterprise and compliance-adjacent teams adopting AI agents need auditability; competitors log webhook calls as 'Bot updated status' with zero reasoning provenance — this is a trust wedge targeting regulated-industry customers already on Zergboard
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires first-class agent identity in the data model so reasoning can be attached to a typed actor, not a generic API token; Linear/Asana log human actor IDs — retrofitting agent-reasoning provenance means changing their activity-log schema and every upstream writer
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Notion data-portability complaints (479 upvotes HN) and Trello account-ownership ambiguity (731 upvotes HN) both signal platform trust deficits; agent reasoning audit directly addresses this for AI-native teams; CesiumAstro (ITAR-adjacent) and DCAIG (defense) are live customers with latent compliance requirements

differentiation_opportunities.name: Ephemeral Agent Workspaces with TTL Tokens
differentiation_opportunities.what: Mint a scoped API token for an agent task with an explicit TTL and a declared intent (e.g., 'read board X, write to column Y, expires in 2 hours'); token auto-revokes at TTL; full access log attached to the issuing card
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: CesiumAstro and DCAIG/Andesite are active customers in regulated/defense-adjacent spaces — provable, time-bounded agent access is a trust feature that unlocks regulated-industry sales; no PM tool offers TTL tokens as a first-class concept
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires TTL semantics baked into the auth layer with token lifecycle linked to card state — Jira/Linear/Asana API tokens are indefinite by design; retrofitting expiry plus intent-scoping requires touching auth, audit logging, and the card model simultaneously across their existing surface area
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: CesiumAstro (aerospace) and DCAIG (defense acquisition) are production Zergboard customers — regulated-industry trust requirements are already latent in the customer base; no competitor offers this even at enterprise tier

differentiation_opportunities.name: Cross-Board Dependency Graph with Agent-monitored Unblocking
differentiation_opportunities.what: Cards on different boards (different teams, different tenants) declare blocking/blocked-by relationships; an agent monitors dependency state and posts a Zstack notification when an upstream card resolves
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Zergboard already serves multiple orgs (CesiumAstro, Andesite, Zerg internal) — cross-org dependency tracking is a natural next primitive; no competitor offers it because their tenancy models are siloed by design
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires a dependency graph that spans tenant boundaries with permission-aware traversal — Linear's dependency model is team-scoped; Jira's cross-project links require the same Jira instance; multi-tenant cross-org graphs require the shared auth primitive Zstack already has
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: The Andesite DCAIG board (60+ cards) tracking external connector status is the exact use case — that board is inherently cross-org; GitHub Projects is the only competitor with cross-repo tracking and it is limited to GitHub entities

differentiation_opportunities.name: In-product Agent-generated Standup Digest (Cross-board, Board-state Aware)
differentiation_opportunities.what: A scheduled in-product view where an agent reads all boards the user has access to, synthesizes prior card movements, and produces a standup-format digest rendered natively in Zergboard — no export, no copy-paste
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: digest.py already proves this pattern in production for Zerg; shipping it as a first-class product feature (not a cron script) makes it available to all tenants and turns an internal tool into a market differentiator with daily active-use gravity
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires an agent with cross-board read access in the same auth session — Linear's AI summarization is per-team; Asana's AI features are scoped to individual projects; a standup spanning multiple orgs requires the shared agent context layer Zstack already has
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: No competitor generates cross-board standups natively; digest.py running in production proves the data model supports it with no new infrastructure

differentiation_opportunities.name: Agent-completed Sub-issues with Parent Auto-resolution
differentiation_opportunities.what: Sub-issues assigned to an agent auto-close when the agent posts a completion summary; when all sub-issues close, the parent card transitions to a configurable 'agent-resolved' status and notifies human assignees
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: 'sub-issues' is in the current whitespace — shipping sub-issues agent-native from the start (vs. retrofitting like Linear did) means the resolution model bakes in agent actors, not just human checkbox-ticking
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Linear has sub-issues but resolution is human-triggered; wiring agent completion events into parent-card state transitions requires agent identity in the event model — Linear's activity log treats all closers as humans, retrofitting requires touching their event schema across all clients
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Current whitespace note explicitly lists 'sub-issues' — this ships a table-stakes gap and a moat simultaneously rather than playing pure catch-up

differentiation_opportunities.name: Board-as-Template Client Onboarding with Agent Pre-population
differentiation_opportunities.what: Publish a board as a versioned template; when a new tenant onboards, the triage agent reads their intake form and pre-populates cards, columns, and labels — client gets a configured board in under 60 seconds
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Zergboard already runs client boards (CesiumAstro, DCAIG) — operationalizing that into a self-serve onboarding flow turns manual setup labor into a product-led growth motion; this is the repeatable version of what Zerg already does manually
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Competitors have static copy-paste templates; agent-driven pre-population requires an agent runtime that reads unstructured client intake and produces structured board data — Trello/Asana templates are file copies, not inference; building that requires a full agent layer they don't have
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Trello's 'minimal setup' positioning relies on static templates as a core feature — a dynamic agent-configured alternative directly undercuts their flagship differentiator

differentiation_opportunities.name: Zstack Competitive Intelligence Board Type (Competitive Review Skill Productized)
differentiation_opportunities.what: A board type where agent-sourced competitive intel cards auto-update on a configurable schedule — surfacing category shifts, pricing changes, and new features as card comments with agent-generated delta summaries
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: The competitive-review-skill already writes to Zergboard cards in production for Zerg internal use; productizing it means any tenant can subscribe to a managed competitive board and receive agent-maintained intel as a recurring-value hook driving daily active use
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires an agent that reads external sources, reasons about category-level changes, and writes structured card updates on a live board — no PM tool has a native competitive intelligence surface; competitors would need to build the agent reasoning layer, the web-scraping layer, and the structured-update model from scratch
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Competitive-review-skill is already in production for Zerg's own use; the infrastructure to productize it is mostly built — this is a feature that ships fast and has no analog in any scanned competitor
